BETA: Core30 AI Agent Tool Bugs & Features Wish List
Hello!
The Core30 AI Agent tool is very much still in beta! Thank you for your help in testing it. So far we've fixed several dozen bugs.
Here's the link straight to the tool:
https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/492fd5bc?md=e7d75255384a492a865ca01132509fe9
The reason for this thread is so you can post any bugs that affected you, as well as request any features.
If you've just joined, Zapier should push your email address to the tool and allow you access. If it's been ~10 minutes and th... See more

2
Daniel Tenney
• Mar 9
I added an empty "deleteSelected" function to the console and it threw a "clearQueue" error. This is the code I added to the console below and the bulk generate now renders.
window.deleteSelected = function (...args) {
console.warn('temporary stub deleteSelected called with:', args);
return false;
};
window.clearQueue = function (...args) {
console.warn('temporary stub clearQueue called with:', args);
return false;
};

Chiara Burns
• Apr 15
@John R I was hunting around for docx/html option too, it's available on the "Build Queue" tab and will automatically apply to the download options on the "Review and Export tab."
To keep things visually on brand, my approach has been to export the docx version, give the code of an existing live page to Claude, and have it output in on-brand html. Hope that helps!

Chiara Burns
• Apr 16 (edited)
@Joe Brown I ran into this issue bc I didn't realize AI detection and Humanize are 2 distinct fields that have to be filled. You'll need a subscription for one of the AI detection options. Caleb also mentioned in his recent update (I'll drop the link below) that using a humanizer other than StealthGPT is going to land you in the 60-90+% range if you're using Winston for detection.
https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/492fd5bc?md=e7d75255384a492a865ca01132509fe9

Caleb Ulku

• Apr 16
@Joe Brown Depends on which ai detection tool you're using and which humanizer you're using. different humanizers work better for different ai detectors. none of them actually matter for ranking based on our testing so it's really what metric is important to you
the 8 pass system generally humanizes by default

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/core30agent-content-writing-major-update
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
7d • Announcements
Core30Agent Content Writing Major Update
I'm excited about this one!
You've heard me talk about this. March '26 Google rolled out a core update that resulted in mass de-indexing of content that doesn't add information gain to the content already published on the SERPS.
So of course we checked the search console for our clients
Category, service, and supporting content pages all good. No significant issues.
Location pages though... Honestly a bit of a bloodbath.
Kind of makes sense? "Lasik eye surgery chicago" vs "lasik eye surgery in buckto... See more
Like
20
14 comments

• 7d

0
Reply
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• 7d
@Joe S in what way? The work to do the research manually makes it very difficult to do at scale. Claude can’t get the data from the us census bureau api. The message is the same if you aren’t using the core30agent - the content needs to add meaningful information gain over the existing results or Google is unlikely to index it

Mark Aburn
• 6d
@Caleb Ulku Thanks for all of your work on the Core30 Agent, it looks great and ai will be jumping in and trying it this weekend.
My question is similar to @Chris Beazley, I have Australian clients, The US census bureau would not be relevant to Australia, but we don’t the Australian Bureau of Statistics which collects our census data.
2
Reply
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• 6d
@Mark Aburn Great question - here's the honest answer: the location enrichment uses US-specific data sources (US Census Bureau + FCC for tract lookups). For AU clients, those calls quietly return no results, and your location pages will still generate - they just won't have the demographic context that US pages get. The drive-time component (Google Maps) works fine globally, so you'll still see distance and travel time on your pages

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/h1-page-title-for-website-homepage-for-various-locations
3
Chris Beazley
19h • SEO
H1 & Page Title for website homepage for various locations.
The different GBPs for different towns all go to the same home page.
What should the Title Tags and H1 be if the home page is the landing page for multiple GBPs ? i.e What Town do you use (head Office) or do you leave the Town name out?
Like
2
8 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 19h
if multiple GBPs all point to the same homepage thats actually a structural problem cause each GBP should ideally point to its own dedicated location page with matching H1 and title tag for that specific town, the homepage should use your head office town as the anchor but build dedicated location pages for the other GBPs so each profile has its own properly aligned landing page :)
1
Reply
3
Chris Beazley
• 18h

1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 18h
@Chris Beazley if Caleb said then leave it that way and trust the guidance, in that case keep the homepage H1 and title tag anchored to your head office town since thats the primary location, and use the location pages for content depth on other towns without changing the GBP routing

Category/Service help
Hi guys,
I have a secondary category "Gas engineer"
Services provided are:
Gas Boiler Installation
Gas Boiler Repair
Gas Boiler Replacement
Gas Boiler Servicing
Each of these have been listed under the secondary category in the GBP.
The search results in the area for each of these services shows the same three GBP's does mean I can merge all into one service page or because they are listed separately do I need to build a page for each service? @Caleb Ulku @Miles Hayat TIA.
Like
1
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 20h
build dedicated pages for each one cause they are listed as separate services in GBP and each targets a different search intent, even if the same 3 GBPs rank for all of them the dedicated pages strengthen your topical authority for the whole gas boiler category and let you capture variations like "boiler repair" vs "boiler replacement" which have different user intents :)

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/is-there-a-best-practice-for-linking-pages-internally-aside-from-the-anchor-text-to-use
5
Stephan Franklyn
Apr 19 • SEO
Is there a best practice for linking pages internally aside from the anchor text to use?
Like linking geo pages to category pages or the home page, or to other geo articles, etc?
If this is covered in one of the trainings, please let me know so I can rewatch. Thank you.
Like
3
8 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 19
the core 30 internal linking hierarchy is homepage links to category pages, category pages link down to service pages, service pages link back up to their parent category page. geo pages follow the same logic, each geo page links to the relevant service pages for that location and back to the homepage, and your main service pages can contextually link to geo pages where it makes sense naturally in the content. avoid linking every page to every other page cause that flattens the hierarchy, keep i... See more
3
Reply
5
Stephan Franklyn
• Apr 20
@Miles Hayat Thanks again, I will definitely rewatch! Planning to go back through the course in general now that I have more perspective & experience on everything.
Is there any sort of limit to how many internal links are too many? Because a lot of the content will talk about chiropractic but also how it helps with back pain, neck pain, and AI will often have a sentence or two talking about another service or condition in it's content - wasn't sure if I should always try to link those to a page if we have it.
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 30d
@Stephan Franklyn no hard limit on internal links but quality and relevance matter more than quantity, link contextually when it genuinely helps the reader find related information not just to hit a number. if the content naturally mentions back pain treatment and you have a dedicated back pain page then yes link it, but dont force links into every mention of every service cause that starts to look spammy and dilutes the value of each individual link. a good rule of thumb is one contextual link ... See more
1
Reply
5
Stephan Franklyn
• 30d
1
Reply

1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 2d
@Stephan Franklyn yes link back to the main services hub page from each individual service page, treat it as a parent page in the hierarchy so service pages link up to it the same way they would to a category hub, that creates a clean two way relationship google can follow

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/purchased-pbn-linksrankings-dropped
3
Colm Fitzgerald
Apr 17 • SEO
Purchased PBN Links....Rankings Dropped
I'm working with a chiropractor and we were seeing rankings increase. I purchased PBN links from Ice Cream Truck. Now our rankings have dropped :(
I know PBN links can be a risk. Has anyone else experienced this?
Like
2
17 comments
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Apr 17
unlikely pbn links had that impact - rankings honestly fluctuate all the time. if it were that easy to drop rankings, it would be easier to just get low quality links to all your competitors. google is known to largely ignore bad link attacks like that for that exact reason
something else is likely the cause of what you're seeing, including the possibillity that it's normal fluctuation

Mark Fazio
4d (edited) • General discussion
Landing Page Redirect
My homepage is my landing page. I have it set up to whenever you go to my domain it redirects it to the URL format DOMAIN/[primary-category-slug]-[city-slug]-[state-abbrev]. Do I really need that long URL as a landing page or can I just use my regular domain url? Its easier to just put my domain instead of that long URL when Im doing backlinks or whatever
Like
1
2 comments
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• 4d
yeah the straight domain is much better assuming it isn't ranking - changing the gbp landing page url will often have a negative impact on rank if it's doing well but we wouldn't do a 301 redirect off of the homepage
2
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 4d
just use your regular domain URL as the homepage and landing page, the redirect to a long slug version is unnecessary and actually weakens your link equity cause every backlink to your domain has to pass through a 301 redirect, simplify by making the homepage itself target your primary category + city through H1 title tag and content without any redirect

6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 7d
migration process is normal, 1 export godaddy content as backup, 2 set up hostinger with matching URLs, 3 QA on staging first, 4 transfer DNS only when ready, 5 submit new sitemap to GSC and request indexing, 6 set up 301 redirects from old URLs even if slugs match. yes after migration expect ranking drops for 2-4 weeks which is normal, then recovery and usually improvement once google fully crawls the new site, keep NAP consistent and dont stack other changes during this window

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/service-area-business-gbp-creation
1
Idan Bubnovsky
9d • SEO
Service area business gbp creation
Hey guys, I need to create a service area business gbp and for that I would have to find a physical office to rent out for an address right? How important it is for that location to have signage and staff in it? I was thinking of just offering the client to rent a physical space for the verification as I've understood virtual addresses get flagged and suspended.
Like
1
5 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 9d
for SAB you actually dont need a physical address that's visible to the public, SABs are specifically designed to hide the address so even a home address works as long as its where the business is legitimately operated from. just make sure whatever address you use is somewhere the business actually operates from cause google does spot check during verification, but coworking spaces home offices all work fine for SAB setup. the key is dont try to make it look like a storefront if its actually SAB, set it up correctly from the start with address hidden :)
1
Reply
1
Idan Bubnovsky
• 8d
@Miles Hayat So inserting a virtual office/coworking space like regus is fine and not against their guidelines? Does the client actually has to pay for renting or can he just input the address and do a video verification from there?
0
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 8d
@Idan Bubnovsky based on Calebs comment SAB isnt recommended cause its much harder to rank, you want a visible address GBP for proper ranking. for coworking spaces like regus the client actually needs to pay and rent the space, you cant just input the address without genuinely having access cause google checks during verification and you need to display signage there. budget for a real rental upfront and frame it to the client as the cost of getting properly verified for ranking advantage
0
Reply
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• 8d
we generally wouldn't recommned an SAB - much harder to rank
because of that, all my team's verification experience is in visible address gbp's. we actually don't take clients on who don't have a visible address because they're working at such a massive seo disadvantage
and yeah, getting a visible address verified means you need to have some way to display signage at that address. virtual addresses are rarely successful these days

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/existing-service-pages
2
Ryan Carlin
11d • SEO
Existing service pages
Hi question about service pages - i have all my services pages that were previously created. They are set up like traditional sales pages, not blog posts. Form up top with H1, value props etc.
Is the recommendation to replace the content with what you suggest in the core 30? or can I add the content to the bottom of the existing page? Whats the protocol for editing existing content to match core 30 principles?
Like
3
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 11d
dont replace just optimize them by adding the core 30 content below your existing form and value props section. keep your H1 and other elements at the top cause those are working for leads, then add the optimized service description with proper internal linking schema and faq below
main thing is making sure your H1 follows "service + city" format, gbp category alignment is exact, and internal linking back to homepage and related services is solid. the sales page structure on top stays for conversions, the content underneath handles google rankings :)

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/pbn-links-to-gbp-landing-pages

Stephan Franklyn

PBN links to GBP Landing Pages?
Would I do a PBN link to a home page that's already indexed? Specifically, the page is already indexed and then I add on the categories, do I get a PBN for it?
Like
2
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 9d
its not just about indexing cause with so many AI built pages on the web now indexing alone doesnt automatically pass authority to a page. Caleb specifically teaches one validation link per every published page including homepage and category pages cause each page needs its own authority signal to actually rank well. so yes get validation links to your homepage even if its already indexed especially after adding new categories cause those category H2 sections are essentially new content sections that benefit from the boost :)

Miles Hayat
🔥
• 12d
hey @Wendy Craven services dont show up on the desktop version of google maps, theyre only visible in the mobile app. if you mean services arent showing in the GMB everywhere extension you need to click the audit button in the extension which then pulls all the categories and services for that GBP. the desktop maps view shows limited info compared to the mobile app, so if you're checking from a computer thats why you dont see them, use GMB everywhere audit feature to see the full services list and more details :)

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/triple-checking
2
Wendy Craven
12d • SEO
Triple Checking
My Client has 1 Primary and 9 Additional Categories. All 10 Category GBP "Names" get listed on the HOMEPAGE? Correct?
Like
1
5 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 12d
not exactly, only the primary category and 4-6 top secondary categories should be prominently featured on the homepage as H2 sections, not all 10 categories. listing all 10 categories on the homepage can dilutes the focus and the homepage cant rank well for that many topics simultaneously. the remaining secondary categories each get their own dedicated category hub page where they can be the primary focus and rank for their specific keywords. homepage features primary plus top 4-6 secondaries, the rest get linked through the other relevant pages
1
Reply
2
Wendy Craven
• 12d
AHHHHH ---- THank you! I made that mistake, once!
0
Reply
2
Wendy Craven
• 12d
one more question --- can you add TWO GBP categories to the H1 Page? "Medical Spa - Primary, Wellness Spa -- 2ndary. Or should we DELETE "wellness Spa".
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 12d
@Wendy Craven the H1 has to be focused on one primary keyword cause google looks for the strongest primary signal there, mixing two dilutes the ranking strength. medical spa as H1, wellness spa gets H2 treatment plus its own dedicated hub page!

Reply

Jeremy Bengtson

@Wendy Craven 1. The H1 Strategy: Brand & Entity Check
Your current H1—"Be Still Wellness & Aesthetics: Medical Spa & Wellness Center in Jacksonville"—successfully follows the "semantic triplet" format: Subject (Brand), Object (Service), and Predicate (Location). It identifies who the business is, what they do, and where they do it.
The Risk: There is significant redundancy between your SEO Title and your H1. Your documentation warns that having the exact same terms in the URL, Title, and H1 can b... See more

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/do-you-make-the-citations-section-an-h2-h3-or-it-doesnt-matter
5
Stephan Franklyn
12d • SEO
Do you make the Citations section an H2, H3, or it doesn't matter?
just curious.
Like
1
6 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 12d
if you mean the citations section on your homepage where you display logos of directories like yelp BBB facebook etc, that should be H2 cause its a major section of the page, not H3. the heading hierarchy is H1 for the page title then H2 for major sections like services categories trust signals etc, H3 only for subsections within those H2 areas.
1
Reply
5
Stephan Franklyn
• 12d (edited)
@Miles Hayat I meant citations to external high authority domains for the category/service/geo pages or general topical relevance (not home page)

6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 12d
@Stephan Franklyn oh got it, for citations to external high authority domains within thats not actually a heading cause its just contextual links inside paragraphs not a labeled section. so dont add an H2 or H3 for those external citation links, just naturally add them into the body content

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/linking-to-internal-pages-outside-of-core30
5
Stephan Franklyn
12d • SEO
Linking to internal pages outside of Core30?
AI put in some internal links to a couple pages that were on the website before I got involved.
One was for signs and symptoms, another for treatment expectations.
Would it be worthwhile to keep those links in there? if so, should I link them back to this service page? And would these be considered sister pages vs parent/child (just for my own understanding)?
Like
1
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 12d
yes keep those links in if the pages are actually related to the service and provide value to users, these seems like technically supporting content not parent or child pages cause theyre topically related but not directly hierarchical, and yes link them back to the service page bidirectionally for that proper internal linking flow. the rule is if a link helps the user understand more about your services or makes them more likely to convert keep it, the only links to remove are to outdated unrelated or low quality pages that dont serve the visitor
1
Reply
5
Stephan Franklyn
• 12d
@Miles Hayat gotcha, thank you!

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/core-service-pages
2
Mark Fazio
12d • General discussion
Core Service Pages
How many core service pages is recommended for the website?
Like
1
1 comment
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 12d
honestly theres no fixed number, build core service pages for every distinct service you offer that people actually search for, typically 8-15 core service pages depending on your niche and area/comptetion.the rule is one page per service that has its own search intent and meaningful SV, dont split into too many micro variations cause that creates duplicate content issues. use GMB everywhere to see what services your top competitors have pages for and mirror their structure as a baseline

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/leadsnap-heatmaps-places-api-output-not-what-users-actually-see
4
Chiara Burns
16d • General discussion
LeadSnap heatmaps = Places API output, not what users actually see
Hey all, I've been playing around with 4 different local seo softwares and noticed that 3 of them (Local Falcon, GridMyBusiness, Paige) are pretty identical in their heatmaps, with LeadSnap being a far less flattering outlier.
Asked the rep where their data comes from. Got "the Official Google API which has most realistic numbers."
There's no official Google API that returns local pack or Maps rank positions at coordinates. GBP API doesn't expose position data. Places API returns its own RELEVANCE... See more
Like
1
6 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 16d
Good catch, if LeadSnap is using Places API data thats useful for proximity and relevance analysis, but it’s not the same as real user-facing Local Pack or Maps rankings. for client reporting, I’d prefer geolocated SERP-based rank data when the goal is to show what users actually see that said according to Caleb leadsnap is part of his core workflow for tracking rank maps and managing citations, so its consistent enough for tracking progress over time even if its not a perfect user simulation, just worth knowing what the data actually represents when you present it to clients!
1
Reply
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• 16d
all results for leadsnap are done by running searches in specific areas using geo spoofed proxies and then scraping google maps for the real time results
the google api doesn't give search results or search placement - no version of the api even gives that info
the leadsnap support person has it wrong

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/spam-calls-on-tracking-numbers-is-this-normal
3
Connor Doyle
15d • SEO
Spam calls on tracking numbers - is this normal?
Almost all of my clients are getting 5-20 spam calls per day on their tracking numbers. We're using GHL for tracking, and I'm wondering if that's contributing to the issue.
The calls don't even seem to be from humans, just robots/automated systems. Clients are getting frustrated.
Has anyone else run into this? Is it just par for the course with tracking numbers, or are there ways to filter/block this stuff at the GHL level (or elsewhere)?
Any tips appreciated.
Like
1
6 comments
4
Chiara Burns
• 15d
Any time I set up numbers through GHL I get a handful of those, but not 5-20/day. Are your clients in a major metro? Typically an easy way to weed out robocalls is to have a basic IVR like "press 1 to continue." They can't navigate a menu. If I remember correctly, you can build that in as a workflow.
3
Reply
3
Connor Doyle
• 14d
@Chiara Burns thanks heaps!! not in a major metro, but that is a great idea
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 15d
spam calls on tracking numbers is super common across all platforms not just GHL cause you know now everywhere Ai agnets are setup, any new business number gets hammered by automated spammers scraping directories. you can add a quick IVR menu that asks "press 1 if youre a customer" which filters out bots since they cant respond to prompts

You need to change all the passwords immediately.
Rotate the WP salts (wp-config.php) to boot anyone who's currently ... See more

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/wix-to-wordpress
1
Tom Perry
16d • SEO
Wix to WordPress
Hi everyone, I am in the process of moving a client's (local Melbourne electrician) website from Wix to WordPress. I have recreated the site in WordPress with some different pages and simpler navigation structure. The previous pages, 'How We Help' and "Why Choose Us' which I have merged into "About Us' and "Services" pages don't seem to have any traffic or backlinks. Is it worth adding 301 redirects to these pages, despite their poor rankings? Thanks in advance!
Like
2
3 comments
1
Ron Gerber
• 15d
@Tom Perry if the site is indexed and getting traffic, I would add the 301s. It also helps Google understand your site changes.
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 15d
yes still add 301 redirects even for low traffic pages cause any URL that exists in google's index needs a proper redirect to avoid 404 errors which hurt your overall site quality signals. the cost of adding redirects is essentially zero so theres no downside, just point those old URLs to the most relevant new pages for any pages with literally zero traffic and zero backlinks they technically dont need redirects but adding them is the safest catch all approach during a migration
1
Reply
1
Tom Perry
• 15d
Thank you Ron and Miles for your helpful advice!

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/mirroring-gbp-services-into-gbp-products-bad-practice
2
Hery Makna
Sep '25 • SEO
Mirroring GBP services into GBP products? Bad practice?
Hi @Caleb Ulku
So, I'm handling a dental clinic GBP.
Previously, the dental clinic used an SEO agency, but it didn't yield any results.
The previous agency listed products on GBP, which were identical to the service listings.
Does this practice provide benefits or is it detrimental from an SEO perspective?
Should I remove all of these products?
Thank you.
Like
0
3 comments
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Sep '25
Duplicating services as products is unnecessary and can confuse Google's algorithm.
Products and services serve different purposes. Services describe what you do, products are items you sell.
For dental clinic, keep services (cleanings, fillings, crowns) and remove duplicate products unless you actually sell physical items.
Clean up the GBP structure to match what you actually offer =)
1
Reply
2
Hery Makna
• Sep '25
@Caleb Ulku Thankssssss
0
Reply
1
Ryan T
• 16d

Reply

@Connor Doyle for categories i means ideally no overlap on secondary overlap , for distance no official minimum but generally the further apart the better, even 5-10 miles helps significantly. for coworking its just a mailbox or hot desk membership not a full office, and for signage most coworking spaces have building directories where you can add your business name which counts as signage in the verification video

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/is-social-signals-just-a-1-time-thing
5
Stephan Franklyn
19d • SEO
Is Social Signals just a 1-time thing?
Or do you do it every month, quarter, year, etc?
Like
1
6 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 18d
social signals are an ongoing thing, consistent activity over time is what builds the entity signals google looks for. posting regularly on the business social profiles even just once or twice a week keeps the brand active and visible across platforms, and each new page or article you publish is worth sharing on social to create fresh signals pointing to that URL

Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 18d
@Stephan Franklyn icecreamtruck social signals are a one time purchase per page to establish the initial signal, while the ongoing weekly posting is the organic social media activity on their actual FB and IG profiles, those are two separate things and both are worth doing

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/keyword-city-in-h1-does-the-order-matter-for-ranking
5
Stephan Franklyn
28d • SEO
[Keyword] + [City] in H1, does the order matter for ranking?
a client's most searched term according to GBP Searches Breakdown is Chiropractor [city state abbrev].
So does it have a big impact if the H1 is something like "Your [City State] Chiropractor for the whole family" vs "Your Chiropractor in [City State] for the whole family"?
Like
1
5 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 28d
Yes the order does matter slightly with primary keyword earlier in the H1, but honestly the difference between those two versions is minimal and google is smart enough to understand both. what matters way more is that both the primary category and city are mention in the H1 and match the title tag, so either version works fine :)

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/high-authority-domain-links-do-we-use-typical-anchor-text-or-is-the-1-the-anchor-text
5
Stephan Franklyn
21d • SEO
High Authority Domain Links, do we use typical anchor text or is the 1 the anchor text?
When we cite it at the end, will that be a link to the article as well so it gets hit twice or will that just be plain text url?
Like
2
4 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 21d
for high authority domai links use branded or naked URL anchors not exact match keyword anchors, cause exact match anchors on external links look manipulative to google. for the citation at the end of supporting articles it should be a clickable link not plain text so the target page gets the link equity, plain text URLs dont pass any ranking value so always make it an actual hyperlink even if the anchor is just the naked URL format :)
1
Reply
5
Stephan Franklyn
• 20d
@Miles Hayat okay let me see if I understand properly: so I would do something like "according to the CDC, blah blah blah. [1]" and then make the anchor text "CDC" in that sentence, then at the bottom would be the citation, and it would have the naked URL (www.cdc.gov/study) which would also be a clickable link to that specific article?
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 20d
@Stephan Franklyn yes exactly right, the inline citation anchor like CDC or the source name is the clickable link in the body text pointing to the external source. the numbered citation at the bottom should also be a clickable hyperlink to the same URL not just plain text, so the reader can click either the inline mention or the footnote citation to reach the source
1
Reply
View 1 more reply
1





===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/help-with-pre-existing-massage-therapy-service-page-new-category-page-service-pages-etc

Stephan Franklyn
21d • SEO

Miles Hayat

yes link the massage therapist cat page down to the existing massage therapy service page and dont remove any content from it. check if its already indexed in GSC first, if it is then just add the internal link from the category page and optimize the existing service page title tag and H1 to match the GBP service name format with city. if its thin on content add more but dont rebuild it from scratch, treat it like any other service page in the silo and let the category page above it pass authority down through the new internal link you just created :)

Stephan Franklyn

2 comments

Miles Hayat
🔥
• 21d
yes a simple service areas section works well, something like a paragraph mentioning the main cities served plus a clean list of neighborhoods, for a fixed location chiro keep it focused on the realistic service radius not every single GBP service area, so main city + 5-8 surrounding neighborhoods and nearby cities that patients actually come from. put it naturally within the page content or in a dedicated section and each city or area name becomes an opportunity to link to a dedicated location page if you have one or plan to build one for geographic expansion later

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/bilingual-website
1
Luca Budgen
21d • SEO
Bilingual Website
Hi, I'm gonna re-do a website for an HVAC company in Montreal. About 50% of that area speaks French so we're gonna do a bilingual website. How do I go about this?
We did their website a few years back too and back then we just did KW research in French and wrote content according to that research. But obviously this core 30 structure works in a different way. What is the best way to go about this?
Like
1
3 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 21d
you can build separate URL versions for each language like domain.com/en/ for english and domain.com/fr/ for french, with proper hreflang tags telling google which version to serve to which searcher. each language version gets its own full core 30 structure with dedicated category and service pages targeting the relevant keywords in that language :)
0
Reply
1
Luca Budgen
• 21d
@Miles Hayat Ok yes thanks! 1 Question:
Is KW research still necessary? Or can I just create the french core 30 structure with AI and let a french person proof read it? This would make it way easier, hiring a french copy writer and kw researcher was horrible last time
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 21d
@Luca Budgen for the french version keyword research is much simpler than traditional SEO cause core 30 uses your GBP categories and services as the keywords not random keyword tool research. just translate your GBP category and service names into natural french equivalents, use google autocomplete to verify how french speakers actually phrase those searches, and claude can generate the french content and a native french speaker just needs to proofread for naturalness, no need for a dedicated french copywriter or keyword researcher like last time :)

There's a common fear in local SEO that adding more categories to your Google Business Profile will "dilute" your primary category and hurt your rankings.
I came across a case study that actually tested this with real businesses — a law firm in California and a medical spa in Washington. They tracked rankings daily across a 25-point grid and only changed categories (nothing else).
Here's what they found:
→ Adding categories did NOT hurt existing rankings. In fact, most original categories either st... See more

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/for-main-services-do-we-create-the-page-like-a-category-hub-page-or-as-a-regular-service-page
5
Stephan Franklyn
24d • SEO
For main services, do we create the page like a category hub page, or as a regular service page?
In our GBP Services for an animal chiro we have several services related to being an animal chiro under the Primary "Chiropractor" category. Would we make it like a hub page where Animal Chiropractor is the target kw and then list those other services as H2s?
Or should we make it like a regular service page with statement H2s from the PAAs?
Like
1
8 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 24d
since animal chiropractor is a main service under the primary category not a secondary GBP category it gets a regular service page not a full hub page with child services underneath. so build it like a standard service page with H1 targeting animal chiropractor + city, use PAA based H2s to cover the topic and link back up to the homepage since primary category services report directly to homepage in the core 30 hierarchy. no need to create a mini hub structure for it unless you have so many animal specific sub services that they each their own dedicated pages
1
Reply
5
Stephan Franklyn
• 24d
@Miles Hayat got it, okay! Since the home page is considered the category page for the primary cat, I'm assuming I make service pages for the services under the primary cat, but then how do they fit into the Core30 from parent/sister/child structure? Is there a service hub page for them still?
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 24d
@Stephan Franklyn no separate hub page needed for primary cat services, they link directly to and from the homepage. so the structure is homepage is the primary cat hub, and all the services under the primary cat, each get their own dedicated service pages that link back up to homepage directly. homepage links down to those service pages within the content and H2 sections, and each service page links back up to homepage. secondary cat services follow the same logic but link to their secondary cat hub page instead of homepage

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/rank-map-3

Rank Map
How is it possible that I look like this right now?
Two weeks ago the same keyword was all green.
Like
1
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 23d
hey @Lalita Sundari what time of day did you run both scans and were they on the same time day cause rankings fluctuate heavily based on time and day especially for competitive keywords
also check if any competitors got review surges cause going from all green to mixed red/yellow in 2 weeks usually means either competitor activity spiked or you got hit with some kind of penalty/filter. run another scan at the exact same time as your original baseline to comparing

5
Stephan Franklyn
• 23d
Just my humble opinion, but when I see it was ranking pretty well and then all of a sudden it's showing a bunch of red 20s mixed in between the 1s I consider that a bad scan. I will typically rerun it but it will usually still be like that, so I think it could be on google's end (I've even tried with multiple tools before and got the same result)
So monitor it, scan it the next day and the next day if it isn't changing. More often than not, it will show a more reasonable scan within 1-3 days.

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/local-dominator-compatibility-with-core-30-ai-agent
2
Ted Tibbetts
24d • General discussion
Local Dominator compatibility with Core 30 AI Agent?
I've been using local dominator instead of Lead Snap, but I get error messages from the tool when uploading CSV files. Is the export from Lead Snap and Local Dominator that different?
Like
1
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 24d
yes the CSV export format from local dominator is different from leadsnap and the core 30 agent is specifically built around leadsnap export format so that's why you're getting errors. the column headers and data structure differ enough between the two tools the cleanest solution is to use leadsnap for the gap analysis exports even if you prefer local dominator for rank tracking, or use claude to reformat the local dominator CSV columns to match what leadsnap outputs
0
Reply
2

Still in the early days here, but already seeing significant SEO jumps for clients. Obviously it's contingent on factors like population density and industry, but I'd love to find a reliable way to ballpark this so I can give clients a realistic sense of outcomes.

Caleb Ulku

yeah best way we've done is to scale base don population another one of your clients in the same space

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/does-the-core-30-ai-agent-produce-the-content-for-net-websites
2
Waseem Ahsan
24d • SEO
Does the Core 30 AI agent produce the content for .net websites
Wondering if it only works for word press.
Like
1
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 24d
the core 30 AI agent currently only connects and publishes directly to wordpress sites it doesnt support .net or other platforms for automated publishing. if your on a .net site you can still use the agent to generate all the content and page structure, you just have to manually copy and paste the output into your .net site rather than having it publish automatically. the content generation part works regardless of platform, its only the auto publishing feature that requires wordpress

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/do-you-typically-include-review-embeds-on-your-content-pages-or-only-certain-ones
5
Stephan Franklyn
24d • SEO
Do you typically include review embeds on your content pages or only certain ones?
Wondering how to decide which ones I should and which ones don't really need the review embed.
Like
1
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 24d
review embeds are most valuable on your homepage and main category hub pages where conversion decisions are made, those are the pages prospects most likely land on and trust signals matter most there. for individual service pages you can add them but its lower priority, focus on getting the review widget on homepage and your top 2-3 category pages first and only add to service pages if they are high traffic conversion pages, supporting content pages generally dont need review embeds unless they are driving significant traffic
1
Reply
5
Stephan Franklyn
• 24d
@Miles Hayat got it, thank you!

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/if-category-pages-dont-have-all-the-services-from-the-gbp-would-you-recommend-adding-them-in-there
5
Stephan Franklyn
27d • SEO
If category pages don't have all the services from the GBP, would you recommend adding them in there?
I've always known I learn best by making mistakes and correcting them, unfortunately. 😅
I've been making category pages but a lot of times they don't include the services I put into the GBP. Is this crucial? Should I go back and put them in there or redo the category page to put them in there?
Like
1
6 comments
4
Chiara Burns
• 27d (edited)
Yeah, that's the defining trait of Core30 imho.
The structure of homepage/parent/child pages is essentially the same as topic clusters that have been a best practice in organic SEO since ~2017 (thanks HubSpot), but what makes this methodology uniquely powerful for local SEO is how the clusters perfectly mirror the GBP.
(and the sequencing of material after completing the foundation/Core30, but as far as the foundation, the mirroring is the key)
To answer your other question, you don't need to redo the category pages, just weave the correct services in
1
Reply
5
Stephan Franklyn
• 27d
@Chiara Burns got it, okay! When you say to weave them in, does it matter if I put them in as H2s or can I weave them into the paragraph text of the content?
0
Reply
4
Chiara Burns
• 26d
@Stephan Franklyn H2s carry more weight, technically. So ideally the service name would be H2, and then you've got a li'l body text describing the service that links off to the actual page. So H2 with the service name, link in the subsequent copy.
1
Reply
5
Mark Farrow
• 26d
Add to the CAT page as you add additional services.
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 26d
yeah add mentions of all the services from that GBP category onto the category hub page, every service listed under a category in GBP should be mentioned by name on the corresponding category page and ideally linked to its individual service page. this is exactly what creates the topical authority, the category page acts as a hub that covers all the services under it comprehensively :)
1
Reply
5
Stephan Franklyn
• 26d
Thank you all SO much for this - I think I got it. Sounds like my current marching orders will be:
Go back and add in all the services from the GBP under that category to the existing content as H2s with body text
Put a link in the body text to the specific service page (parent>child page)
The service page should have a link back up to the parent page
The service page will have a link down to a geographical page once they are created.
Moving forward, category pages will start with all services from GBP cat as H2

Order of operations; Core30 --> topical --> Geo
Looking to clarify and/or correct my understanding of the process:
1. Size heatmap in such a way that the map pack leader is between 60-75% green (video said more than 30%, less than 100%; so not sure if there's ideal parameters within that)
2. Until you match the map pack leader, focus on topical relevance (FAQs first, blog second)
3. Once you match topical relevance (or if you've done everything and still aren't), start building out geo content

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/when-re-theming-should-we-use-the-exact-category-on-the-home-page-or-target-kw
5
Stephan Franklyn
27d • SEO
When Re-theming, should we use the exact category on the home page or target kw?
I understand on the cat page we use the exact match slug and H1, but Title and the rest of the content uses what we're re-theming to.
But on the home page where we put secondary cats as H2s, do we put the GBP Category or the Target KW?
Like
1
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 27d
you can use the retheming target keyword not the exact GBP category name, since the H2s are what users actually read and google understands the semantic relationship between the category and the retheming. the category hub page slug and H1 should match the GBP category more closely for the ranking signal, but the homepage H2s can use the more natural customer facing language that matches how people actually search
1
Reply
5
Stephan Franklyn
• 27d
got it thank you!

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/location-hub-layout
2
Kevin Tully
27d • SEO
Location Hub Layout
Hey everyone! Newbie here.
Wondering which of these two layouts for a location hub would perform better for a personal injury site. The one with all service pages listed out (more internal links) or the one with the images that just links to the location page? Or do you have a third suggestion? Thanks.
Like
2
2 comments
4
Chiara Burns
• 27d (edited)
The classic tug of war between SEO and UX, Speaking Google vs. Speaking Human lol
Regarding link architecture...option 1 (visual cards) would put links a layer deeper vs option 2 (link wall) has all the pages. If i were in your shoes I might go with a hybrid approach to satisfy both users and bots
Link wall is great for technical SEO but it's a higher cognitive load for users; the visual/image version has a better brand feel and reduces choice paralysis by taking users to primary city page
More and... See more
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 27d
the second one with all service pages listed out is way better cause it creates proper internal linking structure from your locations hub to individual service pages
the first one with just images linking to location pages is cleaner visually but youre missing tons of internal linking opportunities. core 30 is all about building that silo structure so locations hub should link to all relevant location + service combinations not just generic location pages
2

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/do-you-go-back-and-add-internal-links-to-content
5
Stephan Franklyn
27d • SEO
Do you go back and add internal links to content?
For example, when I create a category page, I haven't created the service pages for the services that's mentioned in the cat page yet. Do you guys suggest keeping track of that and then going back and adding it in once the new service page is created? (same with Geo pages, etc?)
Like
1
7 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• 27d
yes you should add internal links once the service pages are created, that is completely normal and expected in the core 30 build process since you cant link to pages that dont exist yet. you can make a google sheet tracking which pages mention each service without a link yet, then as each new service or geo page goes live do a quick pass through the relevant category pages and homepage to add the contextual links. google recrawls pages regularly so adding links after the fact works just as well as having them from the start :)
1
Reply
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• 27d
yes
1
Reply
5
Stephan Franklyn
• 27d
Thanks guys!
0
Reply
4
Chiara Burns
• 27d (edited)
I launch with them already linked to their sister/parent/child pages, and launch the interlinked topic clusters in batches. It's more efficient for me then linking retroactively. A URL doesn't have to be published/public for you to designate it. As long as you don't set live any links that would just 404 because the correlating pages aren't live yet.
1
Reply
5
Stephan Franklyn
• 27d

4
Chiara Burns
• 27d (edited)
@Stephan Franklyn Yes. Either like that OR depending on the site content, I'd do all the category pages first and then launch the pre-linked clusters of services together in batches. In the case of the latter, the only retroactive interlinking is category --> service (so those 3-8 links all in the same module on cat page)
bc I think I'd lose my mind going back and trying to keep track of the web of interlinking between the service <--> service pages + service <--> category pages lol

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/should-i-change-gbp-lp-original-headings-to-h3h4-or-paragraph-text
5
Stephan Franklyn
Apr 5 • SEO
Should I change GBP LP original headings to h3/h4 or paragraph text?
The GBP LP I'm working on has multiple H1s, H2s, etc. I've changed so the only H1 matches the Title tag, The only H2 are categories and important services.
The other headings are for: Testimonials; serving Jefferson, Washington, and Smith Counties; Benefits of Chiropractic; Common Conditions; How it Works (3-step process to book, exam, and discuss care plans); Unlock Your Full Potential with ABC Chiropractic; and then the Dr's name for an About us blurb.
This is all on the GBP Landing Page (home page)
Like
2
16 comments
4
Doug Page
• Apr 5
Many novices use the header tags for formatting purposes and not what they were actually intended for. I find many local sites like what you describe although it is getting better. You just need to understand the hierarchy of the information on the page and use the appropriate header tag. Only one H1 to match the title tag, the groups of info below that should start with an H2 and then if info is broken down into further sections within a group, then add the next header tag in order.
2
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 6
yeah you can change those to H3s or paragraph text, you only want H2s for your main GBP secondary categories and main services on the homepage. testimonials about us benefits and how it works sections dont need H2 treatment cause those arent ranking signals google needs to see, H3s work fine for those or just bold paragraph headings. keep your H2s reserved for the topically important stuff like your secondary category names and top services cause that hierarchy signals to google what the page is actually about :)
1
Reply
5
Stephan Franklyn
• Apr 6
@Miles Hayat perfect, thank you! With that said, would I exclude using the primary category in the h2 section where I would list the secondary categories?
If so, I may have misunderstood the training where the main category is in Title and H1, and then I've been putting primary category as just "chiropractor" in H2 with the secondary ones as well.
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 6
@Stephan Franklyn yeah exclude the primary category from H2 since its already covered in your H1 and title tag, no need to repeat it as H2 cause that dilutes the H2 signals. H2s should be for your secondary GBP categories and your most important services. the primary category owns the H1 and title, secondaries own the H2s, thats the proper hierarchy that signals to google exactly what each level of your page is targeting :)
2
Reply
View 11 more replies

Stephan Franklyn

Or better yet, tell me what to do instead, or what I should watch out for from an SEO/ranking perspective. Like
2
3 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 19
the main reason to be cautious with lovable for competitive markets is the client side rendering issue, google has to execute javascript to see your full content which can cause indexing delays and lower core web vitals scores in the 60-70 range compared to wordpress server side rendering. for less competitive local markets lovable works fine and the speed advantage is real, but if your client is in a competitive market like a major city fighting against established businesses that technical dis... See more
0
Reply
2
Austin Delaney
🔥
• Apr 20
I use Netlify for hosting Lovable (via Github) which has prerendering built in. That means Googlebot etc see the full html page and content.

there's some evidence of websites built with tools like lovable not ranking as well - even if you're using lovable html, it's very obvious to google that ai built the site

Should I go ahead and make a category page for Massage Therapist in City or just edit the existing page for Massage?

Miles Hayat

• Apr 8
id suggest create new dedicated category page for massage therapist in city, dont just edit the existing massage service page cause they serve different purposes in the core 30 hierarchy. the massage therapist category page sits above the massage service page and links down to it, so you need both. category page targets the GBP secondary category keyword, service page targets the specific service, and the category page internally links to the service page below it. two separate pages with clear parent child relationship is the proper core 30 structure :)

1
Mary Bevard
Apr 16 • SEO
Categories
I’m trying to understand how to properly handle secondary and tertiary GBP categories on my website. Is it necessary to explicitly mention and link to those categories (like “portrait studio” or “photography service”) on the homepage to support rankings?
From a user experience standpoint, that feels a bit unnatural and potentially confusing. Right now, my homepage links out to my core services like headshots, personal branding, and modern portraits, which are much clearer for clients.
What’s the b... See more
Like
2
7 comments
4
Chiara Burns
• Apr 16 (edited)
Yeah, for interlinking, the homepage should link to your secondary categories. You can also include a few of your key service pages (what you're calling "tertiary categories"; also called child pages).
I agree that it can feel like a bit of a tug-of-war between speaking Google and speaking human. What's optimal for SEO does't always = what's optimal for the user experience on the website. Might have to get a bit creative with it.
Example: Just bumped into this with a real estate client, where the ... See more
1
Reply
1
Mary Bevard

@Chiara Burns hi Chiara! Wow thank you for this. This is great. I appreciate you taking the time to write this all out. It really is hard for balance Google math and human being lol. 😝
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 16
yes you do need to mention and link to your secondary categories on the homepage but you dont have to make it feel forced or unnatural. just use them as H2 section headers with a brief natural sentence underneath linking to the category hub page, it can flow naturally as part of your services overview section. the key is google needs to see those secondary cat phrases on the homepage with internal links to their dedicated pages, but you control how naturally and elegantly you present them so it doesnt have to feel like keyword stuffing at all :)

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/domain-redirect-question
3
Todd Shields
Apr 19 • SEO
Domain redirect question
I am getting rid of our old domain (name change) , new one has been running a couple years now. but the old still has traffic. should i forward each page individually to the corresponding page on the new domain, or just do a full domain wildcard forward. ?
Like
1
3 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 19
in my understanding you do individual page to page 301 redirects not a wildcard domain redirect, page level redirects pass authority much more precisely to the matching content on the new domain which preserves your rankings better. a wildcard redirect just dumps everything to the homepage which wastes all that page level authority youve built up on the old domain. map each old URL to its corresponding new URL and set up individual 301s, especially for your highest traffic and most linked pages cause those are the ones worth preserving the most :)
1
Reply
3
Todd Shields
• Apr 20
thats what i was thinking as well, but there was alot of competing info out there. cheers!
0
Reply
1
Miles Harding
• Apr 20

I'm pretty new here. This is actually my first post to be honest.
I'm in an AI community, and one of the members answered this question for me a few months back with a detailed overview. Here's the 21 slides that will get you to a safe migration from start to finish.
I kept this in my toolkit in case I needed to implement it for any clients in the future.
SEO Website Migration (1).pptx

Don Roberts
Apr 17 • SEO
GBP Primary Category & Title Tag Twist
@Caleb Ulku / @Miles Hayat - I know one of the basic Core 30 requirements is to have the GBP primary category name placed at the beginning of the landing page's title tag.
Most of my clients' competitors use the same primary category in their GBP listings, but it's one of those long, complicated-sounding names that almost no one would search for or even think of.
None of the competitors uses this complicated name in their title tags.
However, several do have variations of the actual services offere... See more
Like
1
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 17
the core 30 rule is to align title tag with primary GBP cat but you dont have to use it verbatim if it sounds unnatural the retheming approach works well here, take the essence of the primary category and combine it with the actual search terms people use which captures both the GBP alignment and the real search terms. the goal is google sees the connection between your GBP category and title tag while users see something they actually searched for :)

Apr 17 • General discussion
Flat URLs vs nested URLs under hub pages?
Got a question on URL structure.
Right now my service(Core 30) pages are flat like /commercial-cleaning-christchurch and the hub page is /cleaners-christchurch. So the service pages aren't nested under the hub (Category Page) in the URL, just connected through nav and internal links.
Should I be nesting them like /cleaners-christchurch/commercial-cleaning-christchurch so the hub comes first and the service page sits under it? Or does it not matter as long as the nav and linking is right?
@Miles Hayat @Caleb Ulku

1
3 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 17
flat URLs are totally fine and actually preferred in core 30, because it keeps pages closer to the root domain which passes more authority. the hierarchy is communicated through internal linking and navigation not through URL nesting, dont change what you have, the flat structure is correct :)

what's the actual business name? legal / dba name? if the new name matches the actual legal name then you'll usually be able to submit an appeal if you have an issue with the name change

make the name change and only the name change

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/who-here-has-had-good-success-with-acquiring-local-sponsorship-links
3
Brian Kiselstein
Apr 15 • SEO
Who here has had Good success with acquiring local sponsorship links?
Hi, I seem to be struggling with claude finding these opportunities. Local chambers are easy, but for local sponsorships, it definitely seems like its a challenge. If anyone is able to give a little more insight as to what their acquisition methodology was to get those, that would be awesome and much appreciated.
Like
4
5 comments
4
Chiara Burns
• Apr 15 (edited)
I think Perplexity is a bit better suited for this type of task. It's a native RAG (retrieval augmented generation) engine, whereas Claude/Gemini/ChatGPT are LLMs that have RAG as a feature. So it's particularly powerful for research. I have it pull me a list of local publications and kids' sports teams. Then I review the magazines/blogs/papers to see if they do native "ads" with dofollow or opportunities to pitch to the editor. For the sports teams, it often lists sponsors or will include a pdf of their packages.
3
Reply
3
Brian Kiselstein
• Apr 15
Thank you Chiara

Jeremy Bengtson
• Apr 16
Use SEMrush to do a backlink analysis for your competitors. this will show you what links are valuble and which links are non follow. Not all sponsorships are created equal

Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 16
yes local sponsorships work best when you find community events sports teams etc in your target city then reach out directly offering to sponsor in exchange for a website link!
claude cant do professionally theres no public database of sponsorship opportunities, you gotta do manual outreach to little league teams local 5ks community festivals school events etc and ask if they need sponsors. also use ahrefs or semrush to check your competitors backlink profiles and see what local orgs they sponsor, then reach out to those same orgs or similar ones in your area

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/warm-up-campaing
3
Lalita Sundari
Apr 13 • General discussion
Warm up campaing
Hi everyone, we’re currently running a warm-up campaign for my company using ActiveCampaign. I’d love to get your recommendations on the best way to approach the warm-up process.
We have a database of around 6,000 contacts, and I’m wondering how many emails I should start sending per day, and how much I should increase that number daily.
I’d really appreciate any suggestions or insights you can share.
Like
1
6 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 13
id suggest start slow at 50-100 emails per day in week 1 targeting your most engaged contacts first cause high open rates in the early days signal to email providers that your a legitimate sender. increase by 25-50% each week so week 2 do 150-200, week 3 do 300-400 and so on until you reach your full volume over 4-6 weeks. always prioritize sending to contacts who are most likely to open and engage first cause inbox placement in the warmup phase depends heavily on those early engagement signals

Do you use this to guide some of the keywords you focus on? I've got one where Tire shop near me and tire shop are the vast majority of searches (1,711 from Nov - Mar), but they aren't ranking well for it at all - I can figure out to try and rank for those.

Stephan Franklyn

6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 13
yes absolutely use the searches breakdown strategically, those numbers are gold cause they show you exactly what real customers are searching for when they find your client. tire shop and tire shop near me dominating searches tells you thats the primary cat to double down on with your best pages and H2s, while the lower volume terms like auto repair are exactly the kind of secondary categories and services to build supporting pages around. use it to prioritize your core 30 page build order, highest SV terms get built first and the lower volume ones fill out your topical coverage later

should supporting content also be a page or be in the blog section?
Like
1
1 comment
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 14
hey @Todd Shields supporting content should go in the blog section as posts not pages, that keeps your site structure clean with pages reserved for your core 30 service and category pages. wordpress automatically handles the blog post URLs and dating which is fine for supporting content, and having them as posts makes it easier to manage and link from your service pages contextually. just make sure each supporting blog post links back to the relevant service or category page it supports :)

Caleb Ulku

Apr 14 • Announcements
New Lesson: How to Size Your Rank Maps
Just dropped a new lesson on one of the most common questions I receive - running rank maps that are either too big or too small
Here's the quick version:
Look at the number one player's top 3% score. That tells you everything
If they're at 100% top three, your map is too small or you need to drop the city name from the keyword
If they're down at 30-something percent, your map is too large and you need to tighten it up or add the city name back in
You're looking for that middle ground where the top p... See more

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/should-i-use-slang-or-shortened-names-of-cities-andor-services
5
Stephan Franklyn
Apr 11 • SEO
Should I use slang or shortened names of cities and/or services?
For example, the chiro I'm working with in St. Petersburg gets shown sometimes when people search "Chiropractor in St Pete" or "St Pete Chiropractor" or "St. Pete Chiro" (Data from GBP search terms)
I also know some places have slang names, like Missoula, MT is known as "Zootown."
Would it make sense to use St. Pete or Zootown in a Title Tag or H1?
Like
1
3 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 11
yeah absolutely use the local slang in your content and h2s/h3s cause thats how real locals search, but keep the main title tag and h1 with the main city name
so title tag stays "chiropractor st petersburg fl" but in your content mention "serving st pete and zootown locals" naturally cause google picks up on those variations and it helps you rank for both the formal and slang searches. dont sacrifice the main title tag structure for slang tho, use slang strategically throughout page copy faqs and supporting headings where it sounds natural :)
1
Reply
5
Stephan Franklyn
• Apr 13 (edited)
@Miles Hayat got it okay. So it sounds like Title Tag and H1 is always going to be more "formal" where as h2/h3s/content can be more informal/slang/etc. Thank you!

Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 13
@Stephan Franklyn exactly right, title tag and H1 are your formal ranking signals while H2s and content are where you speak the local language naturally

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/google-not-indexing

Andrew Lin
Apr 12 • General discussion
Google not indexing
I'm starting to get google to index my the pages Im building for core30 and a lot of my pages are showing this Redirect Error and failing to be indexed. I do have a redirect plugin on my wordpress but I've confirmed that there is no error with the URL redirection within the plugin..
Anyone encounter this and know what could be wrong?
Like
1
1 comment
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 12
that redirect error usually means the URL is either redirecting to itself creating a loop, or the final destination URL has an issue. you can check in any redirect plugin and check if any of those specific URLs have a redirect set up that shouldnt be there, then also check if your wordpress permalink settings are causing issues, also try fetching the exact URL in GSC using the URL inspection tool to see where the redirect is actually pointing cause that will tell you exactly whats causing the loop

Chiara Burns
Apr 11 • SEO
Internal Linking Between Child Pages?
I get that:
Homepage links to secondary category (aka parent) pages.
Parent pages link to services (aka child) pages.
Child pages link back to parent pages.
Parent pages link to homepage.
What's the best practice for interlinking between child pages? Is it similar to topic clusters in traditional/organic SEO where you would create a web of interlinking between child pages that sit under the same parent page?
Like
4
3 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 11
yeah you got the main hierarchy right, for child to child linking keep it within the same silo meaning kitchen remodeling child pages can link to other kitchen remodeling child pages but dont cross link silos, so kitchen remodeling services shouldnt link directly to bathroom remodeling services, keep each category silo isolated. within the same parent category tho yes absolutely interlink child pages contextually like how you would in traditional topic clusters, it strengthens topical relevance for that specific service category :)
2
Reply
4
Chiara Burns
• Apr 11
@Miles Hayat Got it, so it is essentially the same as topic clustering. Really interesting how local SEO and organic are like variations on the same melody. ty for clarifying!
2
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 11
@Chiara Burns exactly, you got it! local seo is just hyper focused topic clustering anchored to a physical location instead of broad keywords :)

Regional and/or National

Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 11
you can set up GBP at their actual physical location if they got one or as SAB with service area covering the region, then build location pages for each major city you want to rank in, for national client GBP doesnt really help much cause map pack is local based, focus on organic SEO instead with state/city landing pages and traditional seo tactics. core 30 is built for local map pack rankings not national organic so if they dont have physical locations in multiple cities the gbp strategy wont scale nationally, you need traditional content seo approach for that :)

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/which-of-these-toggles-should-be-disabled-in-google-automated-calls-and-text-messages
1
Anton Gulin
Apr 9 • General discussion
Which of these toggles should be disabled in Google automated calls and text messages?
In the topic [Update] Automated Calls says only about disabling Automated calls to keep your business profile up to date but it seems GBP UI has been changed and there are more options.
Bookings and inquiries from customers
Keep your profile up to date
Post to your profile on your behalf
Like
1
1 comment
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 9
disable "keep your profile up to date" for sure since thats the automated calls feature that can cause suspensions by making unauthorized changes to your GBP. for "bookings and inquiries from customers" and "post to your profile on your behalf" those are generally fine to leave on if you want google to help manage those, but if you want full control over everything your post then turn all three off. the most important one to disable is definitely the keep your profile up to date toggle cause thats where the suspension risk comes from

yes upload geotagged photos directly to GBP too not just the website, GBP photo geotags are actually more impactful cause google reads those location signals directly from the profile. for placement geotag to actual job site locations across the target city rather than just the center, so if you did a job in the north part of the city tag it there and south part tag it there. spreading geotags across different parts of the target city builds a stronger geographic relevance signal than clustering everything in the center

Hipster Marketing

@Aiden Hardy Good question — plumbers are actually easier than roofing for this.
The biggest difference is there’s no “event trigger” like a hail storm, so you create your own reason to reach back out.
What I’d do:
Segment the list first.
Anyone 6–12+ months out from service is fair game.
Then make the offer simple and relevant:
“Hey just checking in — it’s been a while since we last helped you. Want us to take a quick look at your system / water heater / drains and make sure everything’s running righ... See more

Stephan Franklyn
• Apr 8
Saw you were doing this for plumbers? Just a couple tips I've found (we used to do quite a few reacts for clients)
Start the message with something like Name? Is this still your number?
We found success for a remodeling client by saying something like "Our roofing inspector will be in your area next week, since you've worked with us before I can have him stop by and give you a free inspection. Should I have him stop by?"
You can also do a reactivation to close out some estimates that never moved forward.

i'm not sure how to proceed with this, do i write a pitch, and send it to the local media outlets ... See more
Like
1
3 comments
4
Chiara Burns
• Apr 8
I'd do it on his behalf. Biz owners are usually not going to make the time for PR like that. If he can provide you with an email at his domain, even better. i.e., marina@plumberguy.com so it looks official
Definitely make sure the backlinks are dofollow. You can usually tell from their website though. In your browser, right-click the link and inspect element, then look for rel="nofollow" in the anchor tag. If it's not there, it's dofollow.
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 8
hey @Marina N yeah draft the pitch yourself and send it on behalf of the client signing off with his name and business, journalists dont care who wrote it they care if the story is interesting. for the sponsorship backlink questions same approach, draft a professional email for the client to send or send it yourself on their behalf, just make sure its clear your representing the business and always ask specifically if the listing includes a dofollow backlink cause many sponsorships are nofollow which has less SEO value

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/tricky-situation-many-ranking-chiropractors-use-physiotherapist-as-a-secondary-category-but-are-not-physical-therapists
5
Stephan Franklyn
Apr 7 • SEO
Tricky situation: Many ranking chiropractors use Physiotherapist as a secondary category, but are not Physical Therapists.
They can legally provide physiotherapy, say they are trained in physiotherapy, but cannot call themselves Physical Therapists without the proper licensing.
I think if someone was searching for a Physiotherapist, they are trying to look for a PT (although they may find that chiropractic care was helpful).
How do I handle this?
Like
1
3 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 7 (edited)
if top competitors are using physiotherapist as a secondary category and google is accepting it then add it, google cares about what services are offered not licensing titles. just make sure the website content accurately describes the physiotherapy techniques offered without claiming to be a licensed PT and your fine!. use GMB everywhere to confirm how many top ranking competitors are using it and if its common practice in that market then add it
1
Reply
5

• Apr 7

1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 8
@Stephan Franklyn wait the full 2-3 weeks before adding it, your already at day 7 so just one more week and then add physiotherapist as a secondary category. no point risking another ranking dance when your this close to the stabilization window, patience here will pay off. add it at the 3 week mark when rankings have settled and it will have a much cleaner impact on the heatmap

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/website-w-duplicate-pages
3
Bayley Cook
Apr 7 • SEO
Website w duplicate pages
My clients website has tons of duplicate pages where the creator 2 years ago just duplicated the original service page and then changed anywhere the city was mentioned to a different neighboring city. I imagine as an effort to rank there.
How should I go about cleaning this up? Google probably knows this is just duplicate slop with no extra valuable content so should I just delete all of the ones using city names I am not targeting? Or do I just leave it and move on?
Like
2
2 comments
1
UBuild Lenon
• Apr 8
I wouldn’t delete them but don’t leave them as-is. Fix them. Here’s the play:
For cities you ARE targeting, rewrite those pages from scratch. Unique content, local context, real proof. Make them actually useful.
For cities you are NOT targeting, 301 redirect those to either the main service page or the closest relevant city page. Don’t just delete and leave dead URLs. If those pages have any backlinks or indexed traffic, you lose that equity. A 301 preserves it. Hope this helps, good luck!
0
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 8
yes clean them up but dont just delete them all at once, first decide which cities you actually want to target then either rewrite those pages with genuinely unique local content or 301 redirect the ones you dont want to keep to the main service page. deleting without redirects creates 404 errors which hurts rankings, and keeping thin duplicate pages is actively hurting the site right now cause google sees them as low quality and it dilutes your overall topical authority. prioritize rewriting the pages for your target cities first then redirect or delete the rest over a few weeks
0
Reply
1

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/how-much-does-da-matter-when-sourcing-local-backlinks
4
Chiara Burns
Apr 7 • SEO
How much does DA matter when sourcing local backlinks?
I know with traditional higher DA = better. But when I run even chambers of commerce through a DA checker, they're less than 10. How important is DA when sourcing backlinks for local search? Does the fact that it's local matter more than high DA?
Like
1
3 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 7
for local SEO geographic relevance matters way more than DA when it comes to backlinks, a DA 8 CoC link from your exact city is worth more than a DA 50 generic directory link cause google uses it as a local trust signal proving your a real legitimate business in that area. dont stress about low DA on local sources, those hyper local links are exactly what moves map pack rankings even if they look weak by traditional SEO metrics. high DA matters more for organic rankings in competitive national niches, for local map pack its all about relevance and proximity of the linking source :)
1
Reply
4
Chiara Burns
• Apr 7
@Miles Hayat Got it, thanks! Still good to get 40+ if we can find them? e.g., there's one COC in the area with a high DA, the rest all under 10.
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 7
@Chiara Burns yeah absolutely prioritize the high DA chamber if its available, getting both local relevance AND high DA in one link is the best of both worlds

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/website-migrations
4
Chiara Burns
Apr 7 • SEO
Website migrations?
Quick q -- does website migration = temporary dip in local SEO? It was definitely a thing with organic SERPs rankings, where even when everything was done right (same URLs, redirects where needed, sitemap submitted etc.) there was often an SEO dip that would last between 2-4 weeks.
Just curious if local SEO would be similarly affected.
Like
1
4 comments
3
Jeremy Bengtson
• Apr 7
Yes. unless you move flawlessly where all page structures, meta data, sitemaps are the same. I moved a server with a clone from a cpanel to a vps = zero impact. I moved a wp-engine client to a new cpanel away from a custom build to a much more efficient and well organized site, this site dropped for months, and now has recovered and 5x traffic in under 11 months. But the middle three months were sad.
1
Reply
4
Chiara Burns
• Apr 7
@Jeremy Bengtson Thanks for sharing! I'm thinking it's not worth the risk unless someone isn't getting reliable traffic from GBP
0
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 7
yes temporary dip is normal for local SEO too after a migration, usually 2-4 weeks depending on how fast google recrawls and reprocesses the new site. the map pack rankings are tied to both the website signals and GBP so if the website goes through a transition period google temporarily loses some of those on page signals it was using. keep GBP pointing to the correct new URL, submit new sitemap in GSC right away, and manually request indexing for your most important pages to speed up recovery. ... See more
1
Reply
4
Chiara Burns
• Apr 7
@Miles Hayat Okay, so exact same rules and pretty much the same timeline as organic. ty!

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/links-amongst-peers
4
Scott DeSalvo
Apr 7 • SEO
Links Amongst Peers
Is it OK for me to interlink between pages or posts at the same "level" in my site hierarchy?
For example, If I got from Home -> Plumber -> Emergency Leaks
and then I have 5 Posts linking up to Emergency Leaks and Emergency Leaks links down to the 5 posts.
Can (and should) I be linking the 5 support posts to one another?
Claude really wants me to. Lol.
Thanks in advance for any replies.
Like
1
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 7
yeah you can link supporting posts to each other if theyre topically related like if one post is about burst pipe emergency and another is about water damage prevention those can link to each other makes sense. just make sure all posts still link back UP to the emergency leaks parent page thats the main silo structure. horizontal linking between related posts is fine as long as vertical hierarchy homepage to category to service to posts stays intact

Miles Hayat

• Apr 6
@Jeremy Bengtson your right that anecdotal evidence beats opinion every time. practically speaking the technical disadvantage of GHL and wix is real in competitive markets cause both use javascript heavy rendering that slows down googlebot crawling and in markets like chicago where your competing against established businesses with strong domain authority and thousands of backlinks every technical signal matters. in lower competition markets like a plumber in a 50k population town the platform g... See more
1
Reply
3
Jeremy Bengtson
• Apr 6
Wix hosts the images on a rotating VPS server that adds data to the images and can not be directly referenced. You have no control over the filename or other meta data. YES you can add an alt text and thats cute. but your entire site lives in a system with a monthly fee and on servers you have no access to. Every feature will cost you money from some bloating plugin, you can not control the source code of the site or the page. And YES i have a go high level agency account and YES i have sites built in go high level.
