Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 6 (edited)
id suggest before going the .co route try having your client contact godaddy support directly with proof of business ownership or receipt and request account recovery, this is the solution and worth spending a week pursuing before building on a new domain cause .co vs .com does matter and starting fresh loses any existing authority the old domain had. if godaddy wont help then yes build on the .co and set up redirects but exhaust the recovery option first and if the domain expired in future then you can buy again
2

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/client-acquiring-a-shop-in-a-new-area-website-question
2
Mark Volzer
Apr 6 • SEO
Client Acquiring A Shop In A New Area - Website Question
I have a client who is acquiring another shop in an area that my client does not currently serve. It's pretty much perfectly located between the two shops my client has now, and if we get it ranking it will cover the area between the two shops.
What is the best way to handle the competitor's existing website after the acquisition?
Should we keep it running under its existing domain (ex. competitor.com)?
Should we get a new domain branded to the client's business name, but referencing the new locat... See more
Like
1
1 comment
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 6
option 3 is the cleanest core 30 approach, add the new location as a subfolder on the existing domain so all the domain authority stays consolidated on 1 site. if the competitor domain has decent authority you can keep it live for 6-12 months with a 301 redirect to the new subfolder page to pass that link equity over before letting it expire. avoid separate domains cause it splits your authority and makes managing multiple sites way more complex, 1 strong domain with location subfolders is always better for local SEO at scale :)

Does the same approach work for digital services offered by an agency?

Miles Hayat

• Apr 6
yes same approach works for digital service agencies, you still need location pages targeting each city you want to rank in like seo-agency-houston or web-design-dallas with unique local content for each. the difference is you need to justify the local relevance through content like mentioning local businesses you work with, local case studies, or specific knowledge of that market. without a physical presence in those cities your competing purely on content quality and authority so make sure each location page is detailed and locally relevant not just city name swapped templates :)

3

Miles Hayat

timeline looks solid, week 1 GBP cleanup approach of spreading changes across different days is smart cause it avoids making too many changes at once which can trigger reverification. the gradual rollout from GBP cleanup to page builds to connecting everything is exactly the right sequencing that Caleb teaches. only thing id watch is making sure you dont remove too many categories and services on the same day cause google tracks change velocity, your monday tuesday wednesday split for week 1 handles that perfectly so your good to go :)

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/pbn-links-366ca075
2
Mark Lallemand
Apr 2 (edited) • SEO
PBN links?
Sorry, I am confused, @Caleb Ulku he talks about the PBN links and you should have one on every core 30 page you create then when you do geographical pages one link for each of them as well. How and where do these come from and how can you get these links for all my clients and future clients? Am I missing it someplace in the training?
Like
3
5 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 2
@Mark Lallemand Caleb calls them validation links not traditional PBNs, they are links from a network of supporting sites that point to your core 30 pages to signal to google the content is real and authoritative. you either build your own network of supporting sites over time or use https://icecreamtruck.shop/ to source them, key rules are wait until pages are indexed first usually 2-3 weeks after publishing, one link per service page, use branded or naked URL anchors, and spread them out over time never all at once :)

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/are-near-me-keywords-worth-tracking-for-local-seo
3
Joe Joubert
Apr 2 • SEO
Are “Near Me” Keywords Worth Tracking for Local SEO?
I saw a question earlier about whether to include the city name in rank map keywords, and most people said not to. Does the same apply to using ‘near me’? Should I be targeting just ‘plumber,’ ‘plumber near me,’ or ‘plumber Houston’?
Like
1
6 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 2
just track plumber without any modifier in leadsnap, no near me and no city name. leadsnap automatically geo tags each grid point to simulate searches from that exact location so adding city or near me artificially inflates your numbers and google handles the local intent matching automatically when someone searches near me or with a city name, your job is just to rank the GBP entity and the keyword tracking should reflect the bare service term to give you accurate baseline data
1
Reply
3
Joe Joubert
• Apr 2
@Miles Hayat thank you for the clarification, sir
1
Reply
4
Karan Holland
• Apr 2
@Miles Hayat I thought we always put key word and city name in leadsnap ? Swear that’s what was in the course ?
1
Reply
View 3 more replies

Like
1
5 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 2
best way is to put your audience avatar into the claude project instructions so it automatically applies to every prompt in that project without having to paste it each time. create one main project per client with all the business info GBP categories target audience avatar and tone guidelines in the system prompt, then every piece of content you generate automatically has that context baked in. no need to rewrite after the fact cause the avatar is already shaping the output from the start, way more efficient than separate projects for different content types

@Stephan Franklyn yeah exactly right, use separate projects then take all that compiled info and paste it into the prompt of one main client project. from that point every content generation prompt you run in that project automatically has all that context without you having to repeat it

Stephan Franklyn

Like
1
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Apr 2
no dont create a separate page for the primary category, the homepage IS the primary category page. so chiropractor in lakewood GA is your homepage H1 and title tag, you dont need another page targeting that same keyword cause that would create cannibalization. the separate pages you build are for your secondary GBP categories and individual services, not a duplicate of the homepage primary category :)

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/where-did-dont-publish-too-many-pages-at-once-come-from
4
Chiara Burns
Mar 31 • SEO
where did "don't publish too many pages at once" come from?
I went looking for the original Google source on this and honestly couldn't find one, so curious if anyone here has more context.
Background: I came up doing big enterprise builds and migrations where we'd publish hundreds of pages at a time without issue. Organic/traditional SEO. So since jumping to local SEO through a few Skool groups, I've been a little fuzzy on where the publishing volume concern comes from.
What I found in Google's actual docs is that crawl budget is really only a considerati... See more
Like
1
4 comments
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Mar 31
it's about velocity and how different your publishing velocity is vs historic
if you've gone 6 years with no content, then publish 100 pages in a weekend, google isn't going to pay a lot of attention to that content because it's a pretty clear sign that you just got some ai tool
we have the most success when we gradually increase publishing velocity
2
Reply
4
Chiara Burns
• Mar 31 (edited)
@Caleb Ulku that makes sense as a practical pattern...genuinely curious though, is that based on observed outcomes or is there a Google source behind it? I can't find anything in their docs that speaks to velocity specifically.
EDIT: especially because if someone does a rebrand or overhaul, it's commonplace to stage all the new pages and push them live at the same time. I'd have to believe Google would account for things like that & not penalize
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 31
@Chiara Burns yeah its observed outcomes from testing not a specific google doc, Caleb is right that the pattern holds consistently across real client results. the difference from your enterprise experience is those sites have established crawl budgets and domain authority so google pays attention immediately, but a brand new local site with no history doesnt get that same treatment. bulk publishing on a fresh low authority site just doesnt get indexed and ranked as reliably as gradual builds, thats the practical reality even if google doesnt document it explicitly
2
Reply
View 1 more reply
1

The town (10 miles away) & village they are lo... See more
Like
1
3 comments
4
Chiara Burns
• Mar 31 (edited)
I had a similar question about a city that isn't very competitive 20 miles away from the client, and the answer was no.
In a case like that, it seems SAB is the better option. BUT if the city is already competitive with an address in the location, SAB would be especially tough to pull off. I've seen Caleb's team say that they don't really even take on SAB anymore because it's way more work to get someone ranked.
Best thing would be for them to get a physical address in the city and then setting their expectations appropriately for the timeline in a competitive city.
1

@Chiara Burns makes a good point, if the target city is already competitive then SAB is gonna be a tough battle and a physical address in that city is the strongest play. even a coworking space at $50-100 a month gives you that proximity advantage that SAB just cant match in competitive markets. Caleb not taking on SAB clients anymore is telling, the extra effort required to rank them vs a business with a real address is significant. set expectations with the client that getting a city address is the recommended path for best results

I have asked Claude and done some research and just wanted to clarify my findings. Findings state that you should NOT be using the city modifier when doing heatmaps in LeadSnap because people searching for said keyword are not typing in the location they are just typing in the key... yeah your findings are correct, dont use the city modifier in leadsnap rank tracking. just use the keyword without adding the city name because leadsnap automatically geo tags each grid point to simulate a search from that exact location :)

1
4 comments

Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 28
hey @Aiden Hardy organic website ranking and map pack rankings are separate signals so one doesnt automatically pull the other up, but the good news is having strong organic rankings does contribute to overall authority which helps maps over time. map pack rankings are driven more by GBP optimization, reviews, citations and core 30 topical relevance so keep building those service pages getting reviews consistently and make sure GBP is fully optimized your on the right track just keep pushing 😊

Secondary Category vs. Core Services vs. Services — How Do These 3 Layers Map?
How do
Secondary category
Core Services
Services
map?
I thought it was 2 layers deep after the homepage (secondary categories [also linked on homepage] --> services. And then geo/location and/or additional topicals only added after, if need be)
But there are three modues in the Lovable section of training:
https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=e44cebd15365427d8e70d405be995e1b
https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=3032def6a271461aabad3ca5f5defc86

Like
1
11 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 28
hey @Chiara Burns it actually maps to a 3 level hierarchy under homepage. secondary category pages are your GBP secondary category hubs, core service pages are major high value service hubs that sit under a secondary category which then links down to child services, and services are the individual child service pages that are the most granular level. so the full structure is homepage === secondary category pages === core service pages === individual child service pages. not every business needs ... See more

Reply
4

1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 28
@Chiara Burns yeah the green supporting content boxes are actually the PAA/quora/reddit topical blog posts that support the service pages, those are separate from child service pages. the child services are the individual GBP service pages that sit under the drain cleaning service category page
so the hierarchy from the diagram is homepage === category pages (secondary GBP categories) === service pages (individual GBP services)=== then supporting content in green sits alongside service pages for ... See more

ZeroGPT API Change
Early this morning zerogpt updated its api so the prior api keys no longer function
personally, I tried to switch over to the new one, but it required a verification email which never worked for me
so I pushed an update to the core 30 ai agent to use a Winston API key or zerogpt (user's choice)
If you're having trouble with the zerogpt, check out winston: https://gowinston.ai/
I'm looking at implementing credits in the near term which will allow you to not enter each indiivdual api key (will be your option)

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-call-tracking-number
3
Bobbee P
Mar 25 • SEO
GBP Call Tracking 📞 Number
In the GBP there is a section for a secondary phone. According to Google, you can have a call-tracking number in the primary (visible) number slot and not affect the NAP consistency SO LONG AS the real business phone number is in the 2ndary slot.
@Caleb Ulku what is your experienceon this? (I just want to ensure I can track all GBP calls now). Thx!
Like
2
3 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 25
in my understanding yeah that setup works, put your callrail tracking number as the primary visible number and your real business number in the secondary slot. google uses the backend data for NAP consistency checks so your rankings wont be affected and you get full call tracking from GBP
1
Reply
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Mar 25
Yep we do it all the time

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/core30
3
Andrew Lin
Mar 26 (edited) • SEO
Publishing Velocity
I have completed the core30 framework for my website. 32 pages new pages to be added. My web developer is adding them right now.
Is the best practice to not publish all of it at once but rather publish 2-3 pages everyday? I'm new to SEO and I just read about this.
Like
1
1 comment
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 26
yeah publishing in batches is the safer approach, dropping 32 pages all at once can they might not index them all properly, publish 8-12 pages first week then 4-6 pages every week or two after that, make sure each batch gets indexed in GSC before pushing the next one. this approach lets google crawl and understand your site structure gradually which builds trust faster than a sudden massive content dump
1
Reply
1

@Caleb Ulku is this correct or am I halucinating?
Like

1 comment

Miles Hayat

• Mar 25
hey @Justin Bryce that used to be a concern years ago but google has gotten much better at crawling accordion content, googlebot does render and read hidden content now so FAQ accordions are totally fine for SEO and we uses them on core 30 sites cause they look clean and google can still index the content, just make sure the FAQ schema markup points to the actual questions and answers and your good to go :)

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/question-on-heatmaps
2
Lokesh Chand
Mar 18 • General discussion
Question on Heatmaps
If the physical address of the business is in Ephrata PA and client wants to rank in Lititz PA which 8 mile far what should be
1- the main target keyword on the GBP landing page?
2- how should we scan the heatmaps? How many miles? And where should the pin be placed
Like
1
8 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 18
GBP landing page h1 and title tag should target the primary category + Ephrata since thats where the business is physically located, then build a dedicated location page targeting the same service plus Lititz to capture that market
for the rank map center the grid on Lititz not Ephrata to see how you actually rank there, use 13x13 grid at 1 mile spacing and that will show you the true picture of visibility in the target city. 8 miles is very rankable with proper core 30 implementation, build the Lititz location page and set it as a service area in GBP
1
Reply
2
Lokesh Chand
• Mar 19 (edited)
@Miles Hayat Thank you let's assume if this was a SAB what would we do in that situation?
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 19
@Lokesh Chand the approach is the same, hide the address in GBP but still enter the real Ephrata address in the backend for proximity signals, then set Lititz as a service area
GBP landing page still targets primary category plus Ephrata, build the dedicated Lititz location page and center your rank map grid on Lititz to track visibility there. SAB just means hidden address not no address, proximity signals still work from the backend location even when public facing address is hidden!

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/primary-category-services
3
Andrew Lin
Mar 20 • General discussion
Primary Category Services
From my understanding, the homepage serves two roles:
the GBP landing page, and
the primary category page
We’re supposed to feature only 2-3 top services from the primary category on the homepage, along with links to the secondary categories (typically within H2 sections).
My question is: if the primary category includes around 10 services but only 2–3 are highlighted on the homepage, where should the remaining services go?
Should each of those still have its own dedicated page, even if they’re not directly featured on the homepage?
Like
1
5 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 20
yes every service needs its own dedicated page regardless of whether it is featured on the homepage, the homepage just highlights the top 2-3 as entry points but all remaining services live on their own pages!
the remaining services under primary category get linked from the primary category page not the homepage directly, homepage links to category page, category page links to all service pages underneath it. that is the correct core 30 hierarchy and every service gets its own page no exceptions
1
Reply
3
Andrew Lin
• Mar 22
@Miles Hayat here Elliott says the homepage is the primary category page, but your explanation suggests there should be a separate primary category page that links to all the remaining services from the primary category. Which structure is correct?
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 22
@Andrew Lin your right to flag that, let me clarify elliott is correct the homepage IS the primary category page, there is no separate primary category page. what i meant is the homepage links to all your service pages directly for the primary category services, so those remaining 7-8 services still get their own dedicated pages they just get linked from the homepage either through a services section or nav menu. secondary categories get their own separate category hub pages, but primary category services all live under the homepage itself. sorry for the confusion

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-services-auto-duplicating-across-categories
1
Tommy West
Mar 20 • SEO
GBP Services Auto-Duplicating Across Categories
Setting up a GBP for a painting company. Primary category is Painter with three secondaries: Drywall Contractor, Pressure Washing Service, and Contractor.
I originally had Painting as a secondary category, too, but ran into an issue. When I added services under Painter (primary) and Painting (secondary), Google kept merging them together. If I tried to delete a service from just the Painting category, it would delete it from all categories. No way to separate them.
So I dropped Painting as a secon... See more
Like
1
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 20
yes your insight is right, when two categories are semantically identical like Painter and Painting Google treats them as the same entity and merges the services
consolidating under the primary Painter category and keeping the semantically distinct secondaries like Drywall Contractor and Pressure Washing is the correct approach. duplicate services across identical categories dont double your ranking signals, Google just normalizes them into one signal anyway so you get cleaner profile structure by keeping one strong primary with distinct secondaries :)
2
Reply
1
Tommy West
• Mar 21
Thank you, @Miles Hayat!
1
Reply
1

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/city-in-the-gbp-service-name
2
Don Roberts
Mar 20 • SEO
City in the GBP Service Name?
Caleb/Miles,
I know I missed this somewhere in the training, but is it a best practice (or not) to include the city name in a GBP Service name (like “Water Heater Installation Denver”)?
Or is the city name just included on the site title, H1, slug, etc.?
Like
1
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 20
no city name in GBP service names, keep them clean like "Water Heater Installation" without location modifiers since GBP services are not the place for geo targeting
the city targeting happens on your website through title tags, H1s, URL slugs and page content. GBP already knows your location from the verified address :)

2
Benjamin Shannon
Mar 19 • General discussion
Updating/ Changing GBP Hours
I have two new clients that I need to update service hours. I'm curious if there is a best practice process the pro SEO's recommend? I have lead snap so I plan to create citations and add the new hours but their websites and GBP's both need to be updated too. I'd like to just change them all at the same time but Google has in the past changed hours back or made really weird hours for some of my clients because the updated hours were not consistent with online citations. I don't want to wait a month to change GBP while leadsnap hopefully builds and updates any existing citations. TIA
Like
1
1 comment
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 19
Hey @Benjamin Shannon congrats con new clients :) you should update website and GBP hours first on the same day, then run the Leadsnap citation update immediately after so everything changes in one coordinated push rather than staggered over weeks
audit existing citations in Leadsnap first to find any conflicting hours before you push the update, that way your not fighting old data after the change. monitor GBP in incognito for 48 hours after to catch any Google auto reverts and fix them immediately before they get indexed

Miles Hayat

yes @Brian Kiselstein that shared address is definitely a concern even if the other listing isnt showing up, google sees 2 GBPs at same address as a duplicate signal which can suppress both listings
confirm with client its his home address then request removal of the old associate listing through google maps claiming its closed or moved. get that cleaned up asap before building more authority on a listing that has a duplicate flag hanging over it

Usually the way Google handles this is by making one of the profiles the primary and deprecating any others at that address with a similar primary category. If it were doing that, would be minimal impact. But Miles is right that it can create real problems so definitely a good move to address it

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/rank-map-tracking
2
Joe Smith
Mar 17 • SEO
rank map tracking
For rank/heat maps specifically, how many keywords should you track per business? Do you focus mainly on ‘service + city’ (like plumbing Fort Wayne), or also include variations like ‘near me’ and specific services (water heater installation Fort Wayne)? Curious how broad vs focused you go for a client. Basically how many terms/keywords should you be trying to rank for
Like
1
5 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 17
Hey @Joe Smith track one keyword per GBP category so 3-5 keywords max for most clients, just the raw category keyword without city modifier since Leadsnap geo tags automatically based on grid location
never track near me in Leadsnap and dont track every service variation, just the primary and secondary category, keep it simple, one keyword per category tells you everything you need to know about overall ranking health without cluttering your dashboard with too many grids :)
1
Reply
2
Joe Smith
• Mar 19
@Miles Hayat what tools can i use to figure out what terms people are searching for the most? is ahrefs good? Does google have a tool to use?
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 19
@Joe Smith you can use google keyword planner is free and gives you real sv data from Google, but GMB Everywhere is the important tool for local specifically because it shows you what categories and services the top ranking competitors are using which tells you exactly what Google rewards in that market. Ahrefs is good but for core 30 local SEO GMB Everywhere plus google keyword planner covers everything you need

for UTM parameters keep them off your GBP URL but totally fine to use on other marketing channels like email campaigns, Facebook ads and social posts where you need campaign level tracking in GA4

@Christopher Bennett - I'll do my best to explain my understanding and how I've set it up.
I have Projects for each of the page types that gives the outline, and then I have a Project specifically for that client.

6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Feb 2
outline prompts create content strategy first, then lovable prompts generate actual pages.
workflow is use outline prompts to map site structure what pages needed, content for each then feed those outlines into lovable prompts one by one
general outline = strategy
gbp outline = which pages to build
9 lovable prompts = turn outlines into functioning pages.
id suggest rewatch module if still confused

@Justin Bryce lovable is a new module caleb added for building core 30 sites faster with AI. its an alternative to manually building in wordpress or other platforms and the copy project still works if your doing traditional site building, but lovable workflow uses outlines to generate actual code that becomes a functioning site automatically

3
Jean Almonte
Mar 14 • SEO
DBA - When to use it and Process
I'm working with a Landscaper and I have been thinking about the DBA. Two questions: He's physically in City A, but wants to Rank in City B, would it be ok to get a DAB with City B in the business name? How much work is it for them to do the DBA properly? Is an stablished business of 25+ years. I know the other option is to just get an office in the city you want to rank and a second GBP, but that's obviosly a lot more expensive.
For context, I think we can get him ranking without the DBA just us... See more
Like
3
4 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 14
hey @Jean Almonte dba with city b in the name can work but its risky cause if hes physically in city a and files dba for city b without actual presence there google might flag it. dba filing is pretty easy usually 50-150 bucks at county clerk and takes few weeks to process not much work.
honestly if hes established 25 years just do core 30 with strong service area page for city b and sab setup on gbp listing city b as service area. dba only worth it if you actually getting virtual office or coworking space in city b to make it legit otherwise just optimize for it without dba and save the hassle
0
Reply
3
Jean Almonte
• Mar 14
@Miles Hayat Awesome. Thanks! He might do a second location in City B in the future. Maybe at that time we can look into the DBA. When you do DBA do you have to change logos, etc? Just tryna learn ahead of time to advise what needs to actually happen as far as paperwork and brnading if anything as far as google needs to see it as legit
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 14
@Jean Almonte nah dba doesnt require new logo its just doing business as so he can operate under different name while keeping same legal entity. like if his llc is jean landscaping inc he can file dba as jean landscaping city b and use that on gbp and marketing but same business legally. google just needs to see dba filing docs if they ask for verification so keep copy of county filing. branding can stay same or you can tweak it slightly for city b location but not required its flexible

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/how-to-access-client-gbp
2
Joe Smith
Mar 13 • General discussion
How to access client GBP
how do you get access to a clients gbp? I have been looking around but haven't found the easiest way. Can anyone share how they do it?
Like
1
4 comments
2
Lior Manelis
• Mar 13
Hey Joe - the client needs to grant you permission through their profile and add your email or group code to it. It has to be done from their end.
Sign in to the Google account used to manage your business.
Click the three-dot menu icon
Select "Business Profile settings".
Click "People and access".
Click "Add".
Enter the email address for the new user.
Choose a role (Owner or Manager).
Click "Invite".
1
Reply
2
Joe Smith
• Mar 13
thank you Lior!
0
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 13
easiest way is have the client add you as a manager directly from their GBP dashboard, they go to their profile, click the three dots, select Business Profile Settings then Managers and add your Google account email
0
Reply
2

• Mar 13
@Miles Hayat Thank you Miles!

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/sab-vs-single-location
2
Brandon Bradford
Mar 11 • SEO
SAB vs Single Location
Just watched the "Types of GBP" lesson and most of my prospect clients are going to be SABs...
Got my first client the other day who is a landscaper and is an SAB. A fair few of the other landscapers around him have their addresses listed on Google (these are mostly home addresses), so are single-location businesses. I'd say the split is 60% address listed, 40% SAB.
Probably a stupid question, but is the best solution to this just getting my current client and any future clients to put their home addresses on their GBP, or is it better to keep them as SABs?
Like
1
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 11
visible address always ranks stronger due to proximity signals, if your client is comfortable showing their home address it gives a real map pin which google uses to anchor local rankings
if privacy is a concern a cheap coworking space address is worth the $50-100 monthly cost just for the stronger proximity signal. SAB can still rank well but you will always be fighting against visible pin businesses in the same area, visible address is the competitive advantage especially when 60% of competitors already have one
1
Reply
2
Brandon Bradford
• Mar 12
@Miles Hayat Awesome, thank you! This is really helpful!

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/why-do-we-do-this
2
Catalin Topala
Mar 12 • SEO
Why do we do this?
Why do we use the prompt to remove duplicate services and overlaps of services in the gbp categories?
If it is better for there to not be any overlap, why is google updating the profile with new services? I have an example here with multiple services all about lvp, why is it better to only capture one version of how searchers look for it? some will search lvp others vinyl plank, or luxury vinyl plank etc.
If anyone has any input, i am happy to hear it. @Caleb Ulku @Miles Hayat
Like
2
1 comment
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 12
google auto adds services based on what it scrapes from your website and third party sources which is why you see duplicates, pick the highest SV version for each service in GBP and let your website content handle all the keyword variations naturally
unfortunately you cant stop google from adding services so make sure you check your GBP daily or at minimum weekly and remove anything that doesnt match what you actually offer or creates duplicates. its an ongoing maintenance task not a one time fix
2
Reply
1

2
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 10
yes get website live and indexed first then create GBP, having a website already live helps verification and gives google something to validate the business against. IceCreamTruck works for UK but verify their UK directory coverage first
when setting up GBP dont do everything at once, add primary category first and save, then come back and add secondary categories and services separately over a few days. making too many changes all at once can sometimes trigger reverification so drip the edits in gradually, then once verified link to Leadsnap and push citations through there :)

for a new location id suggest start with GBP first not website, get the profile created and verified with correct primary category and service area before touching anything else

sometimes google suggests a slightly different category than expected during setup which affects your whole site structure, get GBP live and category confirmed first then build website to match exactly. that said building site simultaneously is fine as long as you stay flexible to adjust title tags and H1s once GBP category is locked in

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/core30-content-types
1
Derek Welton
Mar 9 • General discussion
Core30 Content Types
Could someone quickly walk me through off the different Content Types used in Core30?
Service Page
Location Page
Category Page
Supporting Content
How I understand good structure on a website, you have the following:
Your "Root" Service Pages (yourdomain/services/service1)
Your Service Area Pages (yourdomain/service-areas/city-name
You have your Service Location Pages (yourdomain/services-areas/city-name/service1)
You have Blog/artices (yourdomain/blog/article1)
Like
1
4 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 9 (edited)
hey @Derek Welton core 30 structure is simpler than what your describing, its Homepage targeting primary GBP category, then Category pages for each secondary GBP category, then Service pages sitting under each category
URL structure follows that hierarchy so its domain/category/service not a separate services folder and service areas folder. supporting geo content and location pages come after you hit 30-40% green on rank map, blog content is secondary and never the priority for map pack rankings :)
4
Reply
3

Category Pages → domain.com/category-slug/ (Secondary GBP Categories)
Service Pages → domain.com/category-slug/service-slug (1:1 GBP service match)
2️⃣ Do we NOT link to any individual service page from Home?
On Home:
Focus of Primary GBP at top
H2 sections... See more

🔥
• Mar 9
@Bobbee P URL structure should be nested like domain.com/category-slug/service-slug not flat, the hierarchy matters because it tells google how pages relate to each other and mirrors the GBP structure
for homepage linking yes link to all category pages from homepage sections, and you can link to high priority service pages directly from homepage too especially if its a primary revenue service. homepage should mention all categories and key services with internal links, dont leave important service pages unlinked from homepage entirely
3
Reply
3
Bobbee P
• Mar 10
@Miles Hayat fantastic - thanks for the fast response
1
Reply
1

yes 2 legitimate businesses can share same address on GBP as long as they are genuinely complete different businesses with different names, numbers and services etc
google allows it but flags it if they look like duplicates, so make sure each has unique number, distinct business name, and completely different category and services, just make sure McRae gets its own unique NAP when you create their GBP

just make sure McRae cabinet has its own dedicated number and other things not the same as Recon, that unique is what separates them in googles eyes

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/google-reviews-ranking-factor-or-conversion-factor
4
Karan Holland
Mar 6 • SEO
Google Reviews, ranking factor or conversion factor?
Genuine question for the group. I hear a lot of people say Google reviews directly help your rankings in the map pack. But is it actually that simple or is it more that once you're in the top 3, reviews become the deciding factor for the customer?
Like if two businesses are sitting side by side in the map pack, one has 7 reviews and the other has 80+, the customer is going with the one that has the reputation every time. So is the real power of reviews less about climbing the rankings and more ab... See more
Like
1
4 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 6
its both but for different reasons, review velocity and consistency does directly impact map pack rankings cause google sees it as an activity signal that the business is legitimate and active
but your right that once your in top 3 reviews become the conversion factor, 7 vs 80 reviews is an easy choice for any customer consistent 2-3 reviews weekly beats of 20 then nothing for both ranking signals and customer trust
0
Reply
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Mar 6
velocity is more a factor than the raw number - the raw number is about conversion
0
Reply
4
Karan Holland
• Mar 7
@Caleb Ulku @Miles Hayat This is great to know, cheers lads. Lines up perfectly with an app I'm building around this. Appreciate the insights 🤙
1
Reply
4
Doug Page
• Mar 8
For new businesses, getting the 10 reviews has a real impact. After that, review velocity and reputation score are most important for authority and conversion.

Supporting Content Link Structure
Hey @Caleb Ulku I'm just a little confused as to how I should link my supporting content for services and locations. I've watched a few videos, and they seem to have different advice in each. Please correct me if I'm wrong, but as I understand it, the GBP home page links to category pages; each category page will have an FAQ which links out to the support content. That supporting content will link back to that category page, and then the category page links back to the GBP home page.
Then for loc... See more
Like
1
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 7
yeah you got it right homepage links to category pages, category pages have faq linking to supporting content and supporting content links back to category page. same for locations homepage links to location pages, location faq links to location supporting content that links back.
for link positioning contextual links in content body are strongest so put main link back to category or location page in actual content not just footer. footer links are fine for secondary navigation but main seo juice comes from contextual in body links
3
Reply
2
Xceed Plumbing
• Mar 7
Awesome, thanks for clarifying.

NAP Consistency using CallRail
Hey, for those of you who have used call tracking, whats the solution to using this while maintaining NAP across citations etc? Put the CallRail number on the GBP and add a secondary number inside so google sees it? Im just guessing. Thanks guys
Like
1
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 6
id say keep your real local number as primary on GBP and all citations, then use CallRail tracking number on website header only for conversion tracking
cause google prioritizes GBP NAP for local rankings so as long as citations and GBP match your real number your safe. CallRail forwards to real number anyway so calls still get tracked, you get the data without creating NAP inconsistency across directories

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/lodging-services
2
Bret R
Mar 6 • SEO
Lodging Services
I just opened a GBP for a mountain lodge in Oregon https://welchesmountainlodge.com The main categories are Lodge and Mountain Cabin. I found that the lodging categories do not have 'Services'. Has anyone run into this, and if so, how did you execute the Core 30?
Like
1
1 comment
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 6
honestly google treats all lodging and hotel categories completely different, they strip away standard services and products tabs and replace with hotel attributes and amenities cause they plug that data into their travel portal
for core 30 you still need to build those pages on website for topical authority, stuff like group cabin rentals etc. website depth still ranks organically and feeds the entity, you just cant link them in GBP services tab. make sure you fully check off every relevant amenity in the GBP dashboard though :)

Reply

@Gary Magill Its a page like the Intermediate Category Page / Sub category page created so that there is not 20 links coming off of the homepage down into services or supporting content. ===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-audit-for-new-business
2
Joe Brown
Mar 6 (edited) • SEO
GBP Audit for New Business?
If I have a client who Doesn’t Have a page, how am I conducting an audit? Obviously, I’m not doing an audit on theirs, but am I looking at their top 3 rank competitors and analyzing what categories and services they chose?
Or is there keyword data on google analytics to see what keywords are the most searched on the map pack and then finding the sweet spot between search amounts and keyword difficulty?
Like
1
2 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 6
yes for new business with no GBP yet you audit the top 3 competitors using GMB Everywhere to see exactly what categories and services they use to rank
that competitor data tells you everything, what primary category to choose, what secondary categories to add, and what services to list. no need for keyword difficulty tools for local SEO, just reverse engineer what the top ranking competitors are already doing and build a better structured version of it
3
Reply
2
Joe Brown
• Mar 6
@Miles Hayat thank you, sir!

There's another franchisee in the same market under a nearly identical name, "ABC Plumbing [Town Name]" without the dash, that appears to no longer be in business. Caleb Ulku
👑
• Mar 6
1 - Yes and suggesting it's closed from a high level google guide account will work great. If you can't do that, you can also submit a redress form. With those isses, Google will likely side with you
2 - leave them alone and create your correct citations with the correct NAP
3 - Yeah you kind of are but that's okay =) Multiple franchisees in the same area is not an uncommon issue

Hey @Mason Ross , Caleb nailed it, suggest permanently closed from a high authority google maps account for fastest removal and just build your correct citations fresh without touching the old ones
common franchise issue and honestly less scary than it looks once you follow those steps, your client NAP is distinct enough that google already likely sees them as separate entities

Aiden Hardy
Mar 5 • General discussion
Keyword in GBP
Would adding a county name to a GBP name be considered keyword stuffing in the eyes of Google? I’ve seen tons of companies do it.
For example
Plumbers plumbing (county name)
On website H1 Landing page Plumbers plumbing (county name)
Like
1
5 comments
4
Aiden Hardy
• Mar 5
@Caleb Ulku @Miles Hayat

6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 5
yes you can add if the county name is actually part of their legal business name and also mention in their business documents then its totally fine, but if your adding it purely for SEO its a risky safer play is keep GBP name as legal business name only and let your homepage title tag and H1 handle the geographic targeting, same ranking benefit zero suspension risk

Caleb Ulku
👑
• Mar 5
it says to change the title tag - not the gbp name
answer is yes keyword stuffing helps your gbp rank
but google says it doesn't (lie) which implies an update is potentially coming so it stops helping
plus it opens your gbp up for suspension - if you have a dba can you always appeal, but then you need to appeal

2) similarly, older training emphasized publishing only a few pages at a ti... See more
Like
1
7 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 3
yes still add real images, external links to credible sources, and make pages look genuinely helpful cause google E-E-A-T signals matter more than ever
for publishing all at once with Lovable thats fine for brand new sites cause google expects new sites to launch with full services. the "publish slowly" advice was more for existing sites adding new pages, new domain launching 40 pages at once is totally normal and wont trigger any flags :)
1
Reply
2
Richie Cronin
• Mar 3
@Miles Hayat Awesome! thanks for the response here....So new sites, get going make sure all is lined up to the GBP and publish! Existing Sites....Line up GBP and the add slow and steady...So is adding Hm Page content, all cat pages and 1 service page per cat page...ok to do in one go? then add 1-2 pages a week of services, location etc....on going? Cheers Richie
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 3
@Richie Cronin yes exactly right, homepage plus all category pages plus one service page per category all at once is perfectly fine for existing sites
then add 3-4 service pages per week after that, slow and steady builds topical authority without triggering any sudden content spike flags. that pace also gives google time to crawl and index each new page properly before you add more

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/gmb-services
2
Lior Manelis
Mar 5 • SEO
GMB Services
Hey @Caleb Ulku
Recently onboarded a client with 200+ Services across 5 categories. We have kept them there, for now, but have also optimized their descriptions for locality and EEAT tone.
What i wanted to know from you is, in this instance - do you reduce the amount of services? and if not, do you have a prompt that will identify the ones to keep for the 'core 30' system?
Like
2
3 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 5
200+ services is way too many, google cant clearly understand what your primary offerings are when everything is listed and it dilutes the relevance signals
I'd suggest trim down to 10 services per category ma, keep only the highest SV and most relevant services that match your core 30 page structure. use Claude with a prompt like "given these 200 services for [business type] in [city] identify the top 10 most searched services per category that align with core 30 GBP structure" and it will clean it up fast
2
Reply
2
Lior Manelis
• Mar 5
@Miles Hayat interesting. Thank you. 🙏 I’ve often added more than 10 - lesson learnt
1
Reply
4
Aiden Hardy
• Mar 5
@Lior Manelis don’t take them all down at once! Might trigger suspension.
1
Reply

Benjamin Shannon
Mar 5 • General discussion
Multi Location Phone Nightmare
@Caleb Ulku @Miles Hayat I'll try and keep this short as possible.
We're implementing a Lead Connector IVR system for a three-location service business (third location is brand new, still need to verify). Their existing primary number is used on their first two GBP's and embedded across all their trucks, signage, and marketing materials, so we can't discontinue it entirely. Our strategy is to make one tracked LC number the primary contact on all three GBPs with the IVR routing calls to each speci... See more
Like
2
3 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 5
yes honestly having same number across 3 gbps isnt ideal for local rankings cause google uses unique phone numbers as location signal but if theyre already set up and ranking fine dont mess with it now. the ivr with 1 lc number as primary and original as secondary wont kill rankings but dedicated numbers per location is always stronger.
if you gotta do ivr use 3 separate lc numbers 1 per gbp with routing instead of 1 universal number that way each location keeps unique signal and other option is ... See more

2
Benjamin Shannon

6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 5
@Benjamin Shannon haha feel you, nobody signs up for telecom consulting when they take on an SEO client lol
but 3 separate LC numbers is honestly simpler than it sounds, just 3 lines routing to same IVR tree with same logic, only difference is the inbound number. set it up once clean and it runs itself, way less headache long term than dealing with suppressed listings from shared NAP

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/supporting-content-for-geographical-relevance
2
Steve Mizer
Mar 3 • SEO
Supporting Content for Geographical Relevance
Hey @Caleb Ulku! I've created the pages for each neighborhood of my target city and linked them to the "Locations" page. Let's say my GBP is Plumber Halifax, VA. I've created a page for Plumber South Boston and linked it to the "Locations".
Since that alone didn't get South Boston into the top 3, is it best to:
1.) Start building South Boston Core 30 even though I don't have a GBP there?
2.) Build multiple "Plumber near POI in South Boston" blog articles?
In either case, should the South Boston supp... See more
Like
2
4 comments
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Mar 3
First - verify it's possible. Is anyone near your location ranking where you're trying to rank? If not, might not be able to without a second gbp
if it's bee done, then we generally just keep cranking out geo relevance -> your #2 option. We link all the geo relevance pages to the service they're supporting but when we start getting a lot of them, we create the locations "hub" pages that links to all of them
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 3
Hey @Steve Mizer , as Caleb said first check if anyone near Halifax is actually ranking in South Boston without a second GBP, if competitors are doing it then its possible for you too
if yes keep building geo relevance content linking to your Plumber South Boston page not the locations hub, and once you have several supporting pages create a South Boston hub page that links to all of them. location hub pages come after you have enough supporting content to justify them
1
Reply
2
Steve Mizer
• Mar 4
@Miles Hayat Hey thanks for the info. Just so I'm understanding you correctly, what would be the difference between the Plumber South Boston page and the South Boston Hub page?
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 4
@Steve Mizer Plumber South Boston page is a single service location page targeting that specific keyword, South Boston hub page is a geographic hub that links to ALL your service pages for that city like plumber, drain cleaning, water heater repair all South Boston
hub page comes later once you have multiple service pages built for that area, think of it like a mini homepage just for South Boston that organizes all your local conten

Noob SEO Question
I'm really new to SEO and just want to get a better understanding for something. So I'm using the core thirty agent and finding the the gaps that I need to fill in. For context, I built my clients website on Lovable. I just want to know for every new page that goes on the website, do I have to go in google search console and resubmit the sitemap?
Like
2
3 comments
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Mar 3
Not usually for a local site - sitemaps help google crawl large sites. You'll likely be fine without worrying about that
2
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 3
Hey @Anthony Martinez as Caleb said dont stress about resubmitting sitemap for every new page on a local site, google will crawl and find new pages naturally
only time its worth submitting manually is if a page isnt getting indexed after 1-2 weeks, then go to GSC and use the URL inspection tool to request indexing for that specific page. for normal core 30 builds just publish and let google do its thing

I'm wondering a few things: What to keep and what to remove between
spelling differences like "clear span" vs "clearspan" they say both are searched
Different language with same intent like "Marquee tent rental" vs "Marquee tent hire" , or "heated tent rental" vs "climate controlled tent rental"

Miles Hayat

• Mar 3
id suggest keep only the most distinct and commonly searched variations, so pick "marquee tent rental" OR "marquee tent hire" not both, go with whatever your local market searches more
size variations like 20x20 and 20x40 can all point to one tent sizes page thats fine, and for wedding tent variations just keep one "wedding tent rental" service and cover the descriptors on the actual page. GBP services should be clean and distinct not stuffed with near duplicates :)

• Mar 3
I know Caleb has made videos that have talked about this, but I don’t remember where they’re at. What you wanna do is run a map and depending on what product you’re using but in Leeds snap I would start off with like a 13 x 13 1 mile and then what you wanna do is look at the competitors top three ranking percentage and you want it if I remember right I think 60% to 80% for those, for the top 1 to 2 competitors. If the percentage is lower than that, then you’ll wanna drop the distance between your points.Disclaimer this is voice to text.
2
Reply
4
Anil Agrawal
• Mar 3
@Shareef Huddle thanks much. I am digging into the course bit by bit..
1
Reply
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Mar 3
@Shareef Huddle Yep
2
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Mar 3
yes you can start with 13x13 at 1 mile in Leadsnap then check the top 3 competitors ranking percentage on that same grid
if competitors are hitting 60-80% SoLV your grid size is right, if their percentage is lower than that shrink the distance between points to get more accurate local data. match your grid to what competitors are actually dominating :)

Gary Magill
• Feb 21
If you're on a Mac and can't run the .exe, here's how to get it working:
Download CrossOver - https://www.codeweavers.com/crossover (there's a free trial)
Open CrossOver and click "Install a Windows Application"
Search for "unlisted application" and select it
Point it to the Core 30 .exe file and name your bottle anything (I used "Core30")
Hit Install and let it run
Once installed, open your browser and go to http://localhost:3002
That's it - it runs in your browser. Took me about 10 minutes to get working!

yes mirror the same categories and services on both GBPs, consistency across locations signals to google that its the same business operating in multiple areas

View 12 more replies
4
Doug Page
• Feb 28
Personally I think you need to change your mindset on guarantees. Offer to increase the "qualified" calls coming in. What they do with them is on them. Tracking numbers measure the calls and analytics measure the form fills. Show them those numbers each month to prove your value. How many jobs they get is a poor measure as you have no control what they do after you deliver the connection. But you can measure everything you deliver. Only guarantee what you can prove. Your client won't be honest. Trust me after doing rank and rent for over a decade.

have you made all these changes at same time? if you making all at once is almost then yes to trigger suspension, google flags multiple simultaneous edits as suspicious activity you can do is submit reinstatement to GBP support next time make changes 1 at time with few days gap between each edit to avoid triggering

6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Feb 25
@Aiden Hardy then yeah the main issue i can see is changing both address AND name is the main trigger, google sees those two as potential fraudulent activity or fake business
address change alone is risky, name change alone is risky, doing both close together even with days apart is almost always gonna flag for review cause everything changed now you get reinstated first from support then leave it alone for at least 2=3 weeks before touching anything again

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/pbn-links-4
2
Jerry Newman
Feb 21 • SEO
PBN Links
Hey everyone! Hope you all had a great week. I have a question about the PBN links. In the training @Caleb Ulku explains that the PBN links don't really carry any weight as far as authority, they are mostly just for getting new pages indexed. However, the new pages I've been putting together seem to be getting indexed fine without an external link. I'm wondering if it would still be relevant to purchase PBN links for those pages, or is that money better spent on locally relevant backlinks.
Like
2
4 comments
2
Cyril Garitey
• Feb 21
Hi,
I've been doing PBNs for a very long time and, in fact, I use them as much for the website as for Google Profile Business listings.
By creating texts with NAP and semantics relating to the categories and services of the GBP and the site, we close the circle, which gives us a little more authority over geolocation, thanks to these PBN links.
However, it must be of good quality, without being extremely expensive, and have a fairly large IP range if you want to gain much more authority on highly competitive keywords.
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Feb 21
yeah Calebs right PBNs mainly for indexing but they also give small ranking bump if pages already indexed.
and as Cyril said PBNs can also help with topical relevance when anchor text includes NAP + service keywords pointing to both website and GBP citations. but if your pages indexing fine without PBN, skip it and put that money into local chamber links, , local directories :)
1
Reply
5
Mark Farrow
• Feb 22
Look for providers that can create niche edits or pbns with topical relevance they work really well.
0
Reply
1
Chad David Odom
• Feb 24
The point of pbn links was to show google that our pages are not "ai slop". Meaning our pages are in a way legitimized because it has an external link. If you want indexing, use an indexing tool.
0
Reply

Third best is to move the pin back from inside the gbp panel - though this often triggers a reverification

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/loveable-noob-question-incoming
3
Todd Shields
Feb 21 • General discussion
Loveable? Noob question incoming...
I started hearing and Caleb talks a bit about loveable. Is it's use case mostly for prototyping a site. Or actually building and hosting. How's it on traditional seo compared to WordPress, or does it even matter?
I've heard of people designing there's and then recreating in WordPress. But maybe it works well on its own?
Help understandingbthe tool and use case would be awesome! I've used WordPress before but not well...
Like
2
12 comments
2
Cyril Garitey
• Feb 21
Hello, I've tested Lovable extensively. It's good for prototyping. Don't count on it for SEO visibility unless you use a prerender to display your site. Lovable is react vue.js, so rendering is done on the client side.
Search engines have a hard time absorbing unbuilt JS, so you end up with a site that doesn't have any HTML.
Plus, the price is outrageous. It's very expensive for very little. Sure, you can get by with it. But honestly, Claude Code does just as well for $20.
1
Reply
3
Todd Shields
• Feb 21
@Cyril Garitey thanks for kicking off the explanation. I'll look a bit into Claude code. I'm assuming it makes HTML sites and we just upload those to hosting?
1
Reply
2
Cyril Garitey
• Feb 21
@Todd Shields Yes, you can definitely create websites.
If you integrate Claude Code into VS Code and then set up GitHub so you can save your website and send it directly to your FTP server, that's even better. That's the configuration I use today.
0
Reply
View 1 more reply

Miles Hayat

lovable is for fast prototyping and building full sites with AI but for traditional SEO WP still better cause more plugins, easier schema, better crawlability.
lovable great for quick MVPs but some prototype in lovable then export to wp for production. both work just depends if you want speed (lovable) if you can handle technical things code etc or SEO control (wordpress) lovable uses react so needs prerender for proper indexing

However, I have some clients now, before booking with us, they go to ask Chat GPT, and below you can see chat GPT feedback, main concern for me, that Chat GPT is mentioning that he can't find 'Google" reviews for Boatanza, this is very frustrating, how can this be solved, and what is missing here.

Like
1
2 comments
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Feb 18
Chatgpt doesn't do a great job seeing google reviews so if that's your primary source of reviews, the suggestion to get recomended by chatgpt and other ai models (including google's own ai overview) is to diversify your review portfolio - the more places you can have reviews the better
also make sure to add your review counts to website structured data so chatgpt can at least see that

The booking page for all the services is on a subdomain via custom domain setup via the booking software called Trafft. The site including the subdomain is behind/via Cloudflare CDN. Trafft app does not provide access to specific <head>, <body>, or <footer> tag sections and require DNS Only setup on Cloudflare.
Trafft does have a Custom Java Script section but that's about it.
All suggestions by Copilot ended up stuck with a wall spending a few hours on this and finally had to give up.

yes should noindex the booking subdomain it has no SEO value and you can easiest fix add robots meta tag through custom vavascript section or set up disallow in robots.txt

Quick Question on Overlapping Services in GBP Categories
I chose 1 primary category and 3 secondary categories for my client. Some of these categories have overlapping services.

You don’t want to list the same service under two different GBP categories.

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/fb-leads-emails-ending-up-in-spam
2
Jack He
Feb 13 • SEO
FB Leads Emails Ending Up in Spam
When trying to email my leads I get from FB ads, I notice some of my emails are ending up in spam.
Anyone have solutions for this?
Like
0
8 comments
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Feb 13
@Terri Sassone does my emails for me and can help fix this for you (but not for free as I hire her email company) :) I don’t know how to fix it myself
1
Reply
4
Stefan Girndt
• Feb 14
@Jack He the first step is to set up the email account correctly with all the necessary DNS records.
Then you need to warm up the account by manually sending emails to other addresses you own.
Open those emails and reply to some of them.
It would help if you had access to different ESP, so Gmail, Outlook, Yahoo, etc.
Then make sure to run your email sequence through Mail Tester to see if there is something in the content that triggers spam filters.
Some ESP like Outlook are more strict than others an... See more
1
Reply
4
Anil Agrawal
• Feb 15
Assuming you are using a domain based sending email, make sure to set DKIM, SPF, DMARC records in your DNS Records of your domain.
It’s way too late for me but I will post some reference material here tomorrow sometime.
1
Reply
4
Anil Agrawal
• Feb 16
@Jack He besides what @Stefan Girndt mentioned, you should get SPF, DKIM, DMARC records from your email sender/Inbox account platform and add them to your DNS records.
Here are some resources to help with testing and setting up things. I hope I am not overwhelming you... just take in what you need to start...
https://support.dmarcreport.com/support/solutions/articles/5000873939-determine-alignment-status-spf-dkim-dmarc-per-source
https://postmarkapp.com/guides/dkim
https://www.cloudflare.com/learning/dns/dns-records/dns-dkim-record/... See more

Anil Agrawal
Feb 15 • SEO
Robots.txt Error - Syntax not understood in Google Search Console
Wondering if anybody has any clues as to why I am seeing this error under Google Search Console (GSC) under Settings --> robots.txt. The error is "Error - Syntax not understood (line 29)"... see the attached screenshot. IIRC, it was either my SEO plugin or some other ways (I don't recall adding it manually), this line/section was added to allow AI bots to search and crawl but not use the content for training purposes or something along those lines.
User-agent: *
Content-Signal: search=yes,ai-train... See more
Like
1
10 comments
4
Doug Page
• Feb 15 (edited)
Content-Signal is a CloudFlare directive. It's not officially recognized by Google. This works if your site is routed through CloudFlare. If not, remove all 3 lines for now.
1
Reply
4
Anil Agrawal
• Feb 15
Yes, my site is routed via Cloudflare. Thanks a bunch @Doug Page
0
Reply
4
Doug Page
• Feb 15
@Anil Agrawal Just so you understand, that directive is optional which means the LLM crawlers don't need to respect it.
1
Reply
4
Anil Agrawal
• Feb 16
@Doug Page I see. Got it. Interesting 🤔
0
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Feb 16
content signal is non-standard syntax thats why GSC throwing error id say remove that line completely and if using cloudflare, block AI bots through way cleaner than robots.txt. or add proper manually and resubmit robots.txt in GSC after fixing
0

Doesn'... See more
Like
2
4 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Feb 14
Hey @Peter Ball pages are sequenced by due dates in the deliverables tab - project manager assigns order based on priority. the spreadsheet itself is the plan. scope notes on client info sheet feeds into deliverables overview.
phase tracking is done by looking at status column across all deliverables - planned/in production = early phase, delivered/live = later phase. for 100+ clients just use filters by client, status and due date to manage views and completed clients get moved to the archive tab to keep the active sheets lean.. gets cumbersome at scale but filtering keeps it manageable

@Peter Ball yeah the sheet is just the tracking layer for internal process but for phase tracking, deliverable types tell you where client is - GBP and landing pages = core 30, location pages = geo phase. GBP Setup Best Practices – Avoiding GBP Verification

Questions:
What's your approach when setting up and populating a newly created GBP with substantial information changes?

2
1 comment

Miles Hayat

its just simple steup initially fill out all basic info first (name, address, phone, hours, description, categories) before verification so its complete when verified. after verification wait 1 weeks then add services gradually, photos, get first few reviews. dont change primary category or business name after verification thats what triggers reverification most. pace doesnt matter much for new GBP just avoid changing core identity info

Arlen Jenks

Google ReVerifi
Well, I just triggered my first GBP reverification. I think I got a little too aggressive with the changes—updated the business description, added several secondary categories, added special hours, and adjusted some other settings. Had to send the client an email explaining he needs to re-verify. Felt pretty stupid. 😕 Owner is not happy.
Like
1
5 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Feb 12
Happens to all of us at least once don’t beat yourself up.
Multiple big edits at once can trigger reverification. Best practice is spacing changes out over 1 or 2 days and avoiding stacking category + core info updates together this is just part of learning the GBP game 👍

Pat Friedl
• Feb 12
You shouldn't need redirects if you're linking everything to the home page at "/". I have a lot of custom values, one being the site main link. So if you use that custom value everywhere you're linking to the home page, you should be golden.

Feb 10 • SEO
How do you handle it when a GBP category and its main service are basically the same thing?
Take "Bathroom Remodeler" (GBP category) and "Bathroom Remodeling" (the core service within that category). A few things we're trying to figure out:
Homepage services section — do you label it "Bathroom Remodeler" or "Bathroom Remodeling"?
Page structure — do you create both a "Bathroom Remodeler" category page AND a separate "Bathroom Remodeling" service page, or just one? If one, which?
Homepage link — whichever you label it on the homepage, which page does it link to?
It feels redundant having both, but we don't want to miss an SEO opportunity either. How are others handling this?

id suggest just use 1 page titled "Bathroom Remodeling [city]" cause google understands remodeler and remodeling are same intent and homepage links to that one page, then under it build specific service pages like kitchen remodel etc and dont create duplicate pages for basically same thing, consolidate and build sub services underneath :)

2
Charles Rosseel
• Feb 6
@Christian Wert This is a paradigm shift in Local SEO. If reaching the top of the Google Map Pack no longer generates calls, why spend so much time, effort, and money to get there? Winning a top spot used to mean something: new leads and revenue. Now it's a Pyrrhic victory, i.e., the juice is no longer worth the squeeze.
Calls have dried up for my clients; top organic rankings are little more than branding. For new calls, it appears Google Ads and AI Search are the new battlefields.
0
Reply
3
Christian Wert
• Feb 6
The question now is how much that "branding" of being at the top actually helps with:
Trust
AI Results
One thing I have noticed is that when I get the client ranking at the top of organic and local they seem to get a LOT MORE AI results.

How to Price SEO for Clients With Multiple Service Types
When a client offers multiple related services (e.g., a remodeler doing kitchen, bathroom, and basement remodeling), do you:
Price it as one engagement covering all services?
Create separate pricing tiers for each service type?
And if competition varies between services—say bathroom remodeling is more competitive than kitchen remodeling in their market—do you run separate grid maps with different settings for each to hit your ranking targets (Top player's top 3% @ 70-90%)?
Do we need to do a core 30... See more
Like
1
4 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Feb 2
price as 1 engagement covering all services. One core 30 for primary category, other services supporting pages underneath.
run separate rank maps per service to see competition gaps, but execute ONE strategy. More services = more pages = adjust price accordingly and price by total scope (core 30 + citations + reviews + location pages)
check pricing sheet https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=7ecaadb4be6a4ab19a54f38a0ee616a1

Question on Chamber of Commerce Backlinks
When it comes to local SEO, how should we think about Chamber of Commerce backlinks?
Specifically:
Is a single local Chamber of Commerce backlink enough from a rankings perspective, or is there value in getting multiple Chamber-related links (city chamber, county chamber, regional chamber, etc.)?
Are Chamber of Commerce links considered a ranking factor on their own, or more of a trust/validation signal where one solid link does the job?
Curious how others are approaching this in practice.

Extremely powerful in my agency’s case. Out of any backlink we purchased it was definitely the most ranking change!

@Aiden Hardy nice! Did you find more map movement with more chamber links? Do you think it’s worth getting more than 1? If so, how far apart did you space purchasing them? And any other links worth considering for local?

6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Feb 1
chamber links worth it but 1 solid link does the job and 1 chamber (city level) = enough for trust signal. dont chase county + regional + city all at once cause google sees consistency1 chamber says "hey this business is real here." 10 chamber links looks forced but you can also get city chamber link and after 2-3 months ranking improves, THEN consider county if you want broader reach

@Aiden Hardy speaking from experience, this can be a nightmare! The only way I found this can work is if you have CRM access. I insisted it all went through my high level, then they had KPI’s they had to maintain like response times, numbers of times they try to call etc etc. it needed to be quite heavily managed.

Reply
4
Doug Page
• Feb 1 (edited)
I think you answered your question in the subject line of this post. You can track "leads". Once you've got the qualified leads into the clients hands, it's up to their team to convert those leads into clients (jobs). That's on them, not you. There are some conversion indicators that you can suggest they improve to be able to do a better job like a professional website, landing pages and reputation scores. But they are the ones that actually speak/sell to the prospect.

6
Miles Hayat

• Feb 3
yeah PBN help with both indexing and avoiding AI slop categorization so theyre still useful even if pages already indexed. they signal to google these are real valuable pages not just AI content. for anchor text use branded or naked URLs like "visit Vital Plumbing" or just the URL itself, dont use exact match keywords cause that looks spammy main purpose is showing google your pages have authoruty beyond just being on your site

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/pbn-2
2
Marjohn Oviedo
Jan 29 • General discussion
PBN
is there a suggested number of PBN links to get per core 30 build out? i have multiple locations/gbp's so i want to understand whats a good distrubution.
Like
2
7 comments
4
Doug Page
• Jan 29
The concept of using the PBN links for the Core 30 is to show some external verification of the page they are linking to so that the page gets and stays indexed. The suggestion, therefore, is to add one to each page that is created.
1
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Jan 29
1 PBN link per page you create thats the distribution cause it shows external auithorty that page exists, helps it get indexed faster. one link per new service page, one per location page. keeps it natural, not spammy.
for multiple GBPs/locations, spread em out dont blast all PBN links to homepage distribute across service pages so each gets that indexing boost one per page
1
Reply
2

Jorge Cedeno

1 comment

Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jan 31
generic city pages are seen by google as spam since august '25
when we do geo relevance, we hyper focus on geographic locations where the business is close to ranking - we don't randomly product a bunch of city pages
city pages do make sense for ads though but depending on how many you're generating, I'd consider adding a noindex tag to them

Daniel Greer
Jan 30 (edited) • SEO
Homepage vs. Service Page Keyword Cannibalization
For a personal injury attorney that we're working with, we have the GBP going to the homepage which is domain.com
then they have the services for Personal Injury, Employment and Workers' Comp nested under services...so its domain.com/services/listed-service
specifically for personal injury it's domain.com/services/personal-injury-lawyers-in-pasadena-ca/
Right now, the keyword targeted for each is:
home: personal injury lawyer
/Services/: personal injury lawyers in pasadena ca
Is this going to cause ca... See more

2
1 comment
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Jan 30
yeah thats cannibalization cause both homepage and service page target same keyword "personal injury lawyer pasadena". google gets confused which page to rank.
fix it like this homepage targets primary category without city like "Personal Injury Lawyer" or "Personal Injury Attorney", then /services/personal-injury-pasadena/ targets the local version with city.
or better yet for core 30 structure, homepage should be "Personal Injury Lawyer Pasadena" as primary, then category pages for case types li... See more

Doug Page
• Jan 29
Although not as complete as the prompt, I also use simple Google searches for quickly finding reddit info:
[search term] [location] site:reddit.com
The search term can be a niche, business name, etc. If using multiple words in the search term, surround them with quotes. The location can be a city, county, etc.

map pack needs 1 anchor location id suggest pick primary township for core 30 then set your service area to cover the whole county and on website make that primary township the main landing page, add secondary pages for nearby towns if you want. clients think limiting to 1 township means losing leads but service area catches all that overflow traffic and in that youre not turning people away, youre just giving google one clear location to anchor rankings from

Local SEO vs Map Pack
I’m hearing/ reading stuff like
Website content, backlinks etc are primarily used to rank in SERP but has no significant correlation with map pack ranking/ getting more calls.
Essentially for the goal of dominating local map pack your website/ business only needs to hit a certain trust level with Google (citations, NAP consistency, core 30).

@Peter Ball id suggest shift to phase 3 when you hit 30-40% green coverage on rank map, not by individual keyword ranking so yeah if plumber houston hits top 3 but drain cleaning houston is still ranking 10-15, you can start building neighborhood pages for plumber while finishing service pages for drain just cechk rank map percentage not individual keywords. once you got 30-40% green across the board move tto geographic expansion

Core 30

Stefan Girndt
• Jan 16
The service on the homepage should link to the corresponding service page. All of them are part of the core 30. @Caleb Ulku named them core "30" because they usually should be around that number. Each category and service page, no matter how many, is part of the core 30.

@Marjohn Oviedo OK, I didn't realize that you were talking about a multi location site. But it's still the same. In this case you have a core 30 for each location and another core 30 without geo modifier. The pages should link only inside each silo (location or non-location)

Although this topic has been discussed many times before, I would like to return to the subject of keyword research for rank tracking. For a city with a larger population (in our case, Vienna), everything is clear: category + city and maybe service + city.
But what about rural areas? Three small towns within a 20 km radius, all interesting and important. The company is located in town A, where category + city works very well. But we should also be found in locations B and C.

Hey @Heinrich Taitl for rural areas track BOTH primary cat + city A where youre located and secondary cat+ city B "cat + city C" service areas you want to expand dont just track category without city, you lose local intent data city specific keywords show you progress in each market separately so yes track category + city for each town that tells you real story of where youre winning and where you need work

Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jan 16
are there any competitors ranking across the cities you're targeting? it's generally more difficult to rank across different cities and I usually look for examples where it's been done before diving in
if your area is full of a bunch of small towns and few people search for the specific city name, this is also a scenario where I might use a "near me" search to more effectively track
1
Reply
2

• Jan 16
@Caleb Ulku ok, tks, will check this out
0
Reply

Heinrich Taitl

Yeah basically. Excpet with local, you rarely need the traditional high quality links. Google is looking for trust that 1) that business exists as an entity 2) that business can do that service and 3) that busienss can do that service at that location
hence foundational links and local links

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/increasing-geographic-reach
4
Scott DeSalvo
Jan 5 • SEO
Increasing Geographic Reach
When expanding our geo reach, and do our search for big streets/intersections or landmarks, do we add these to our exisiting service plus geo pages or create new pages with [service keyword] + [landmark]?
Thanks for insight.
Like
2
4 comments
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Jan 5
Add to existing service + geo pages - H2s like "Tree Removal Near [Landmark]" + map embed + local mentions.
Don't create new "[Service] [Landmark]" pages CAn dilutes authority thin content and looks spammy (Doorway Pages) risk.
Landmarks boost relevance/proximity signals on your Core 30 pages

Reply
4
Stefan Girndt
• Jan 6
@Miles Hayat OK, I'm confused because @Caleb Ulku said in phase 3 explicitly to build out those extra location / landmark pages to increase geo-relevance.
0
Reply
6
Miles Hayat
🔥
• Jan 6
@Stefan Girndt yeah I see the confusion Caleb's Phase 3 geo expansion does include landmark/neighborhood pages, but the key is how you do it.
like create dedicated geo pages like "Tree Removal in [Neighborhood]" or "Tree Removal Near [Landmark] these are full pages targeting areas with unique content, maps, local photos, etc
That's the geo-relevance means expands your footprint beyond core city.
What to avoid i means is don't spam new thin pages for every random street like Tree Removal 5th & Main or duplicate service pages just swapping landmarks let me know if thats make sense to you :)

Shaun Gambardella
Dec '25 • SEO
Indexing Lovable Website
Hi Everyone,
On one of the weekly calls it was stated that Google has a hard time finding websites built with Lovable . I found out that for the most part on marketing websites it uses React SPA (Single Page Application) technology which is great visually but it does not natively render the HTML for Google, Bing, etc. to index your pages. I figured out a prompt to fix the issue. Paste the prompt below into your lovable project and after re-index your website on Google Search Console and Bing Webm... See more
