===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/link-from-gbp-to-homepage
2
Heinrich Taitl
Jul '25 • SEO
Link from GBP to Homepage
i have read a lot here about the link from the google business profile to the homepage and the formula from caleb: Homepage = primary GBP category + city.
the primary category for us is: gcid:internet_marketing_service
so, do i have to have this as title tag or/and as h1 on the page? this is actually not the wording i want to use. any other solution that works?
Like
0
2 comments
3
Jason Leibler
• Jul '25
Your home page URL doesn't need to match your GBP primary category and city, it should just be the name of the business.
Then you'll have your title tag and your H1 on your home page match your primary GBP category + city. It doesn't have to be exact, make it sound natural.
0
Reply
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jul '25
If your primary category is internet marketing service, then I would strongly advice including that exact phrase on the title tag and H1 tag.
If you don't like that, there are quite a few other primary categories that are semantically related but use different words.
The other option is to retheme it
0
Reply
1

Most of the listings I looked at had just one GBP category; however, a few had this many. Take it for what it's worth. If they offer these services it may be worth adding them.

3 - Each URL should have one target keyword. For the GBP landing it should be the "primary category city name"
4 - That will need to be an estimate unfortunately. You could screaming frog and remove URLs with MLS listings
5 - Follow the training. We start by building the core 30, then topical relevance, then geographic relevance based o... See more

Caleb Ulku

8 - I'd suggest reaching out to gmb everywhere. You need to go into the basic audit. Unfortunately, I can't provide support for tools
9 - for local seo, we almost always are trying to rank the gbp and not the url. If your target keyword doesn't show a local map, then you're trying to rank the url

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/quick-question-re-theming-gbp-category
1
Hassan Chaaban
Jul '25 • SEO
Quick question - Re-theming GBP Category
When re-theming a GBP category, does the meta description follow the original GBP category or the re-themed one?
Also I see the blurb on the bottom of the GBP re-theming page mention schema, but the found part which says "add structured data that reinforces your category choices" to be vague; doesn't tell me WHICH category choice to base the page's schema off of (the original or re-themed?). I'm probably misunderstanding, though.
Thanks in advance ✌️
Like
0
1 comment
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jul '25
@Hassan Chaaban If you're re-theming, focus everything on what you actually ARE. Meta description, schema, content - all target the re-themed version.
The GBP category is just what you had to settle for. Your webpage tells Google what you actually do.
Don't try to hedge between both. Pick one and commit to it. =)
1
Reply
1

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/max-of-gbp-categories-wed-recommend
2
Kyle Huggins
Jul '25 • SEO
Max # of GBP Categories We'd Recommend?
I know we tend to recommend 4-5 GBP Cats for our clients and this typically is because they only have 1 (and we're trying to give them more coverage).
That said, Google permits up to 10 and I had a prospect ask why not try to max out at 10. I didn't feel like I had a solid answer that I could confidently stand behind.
So is there any reason why we might advise against maxing out at 10 GBP Categories or even going for the 7-8 range? If so, I'm looking to understand the rationale so I can better field that question if it comes up again. Thanks all!
Like
0
1 comment
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jul '25
The main reason I'd say is that it's often difficult to find more and more categories that are actually semantically relevant. If you have 10? Great, go for it. You'll of course need to create a page for each one
But don't add categories that aren't semantically relevant. If you're a house painter, don't add painting studio and other garbage like that
1
Reply
1

Step-by-Step: GBP Setup with Text Verification
Add NAP to the Website

Use the exact business name, address, phone, and website URL.
Connect it to the existing Instagram account (must be a business/creator account).
Set up a Yelp, Bing Places, or BBB listing, twitter, reddit USE... See more

7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jun '25
@Will Schatz Mobile kids party bus should verify fine as legitimate business.
Don't do service area business if you don't have to. Much harder to rank than visible address.
Get cheap office space or use home address if you want decent rankings. SAB takes 30-50% more effort to rank well.
For verification timing, follow the video verification training process. Main thing is having consistent NAP and turning off automated calls to avoid suspension.
The business being new isn't the problem - it's the SAB setup that'll hurt rankings.

All of them real plumbers, with a real location, and a license. We were really struggling getting GBP's verified until we implemented the steps in the classroom.

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/hvacair-conditioning-in-australia
2
Belynda Scarsbrook
Jun '25 • SEO
HVAC/Air Conditioning in Australia
Hi all, new member here. I'm based in Australia and have just started working with an air conditioning contractor (primary GBP category). Their secondary categories are HVAC contractor, Central heating service, furnace repair service, heating equipment supplier, air conditioning repair shop, and air conditioning system supplier.
In Australia, we don't really use terms like HVAC, furnace, or even "contractor". Central heating and cooling is more commonly referred to as "ducted". The last person to... See more
Like
0
6 comments
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jun '25
I'd suggest using GMB Everywhere to see all the categories and services your compeitors are using in the area.
If the categories don't match the commonly searched terms, I'd also suggest re-theming the categories to match what users are typing into search
The goal with the core 30 is to establish topical relevance. Use local language that makes sense to the people in your area
Don't get overwhelmed. It doesn't need to be perfect!
Also, in general, try to avoid repeating categories as services
1
Reply
2
Belynda Scarsbrook
• Jun '25 (edited)
Thank you @Caleb Ulku - I used GMB everywhere and looked at what competitors are doing. Some are using only the primary category, while others are using the standard secondary categories with US terminology.
When you say "re-theming" the categories, do you mean creating custom categories that are more aligned with AU search terms? I don't see the competitors doing this, so I don't want to do anything that might confuse Google? Or try to reinvent the wheel 🤣
0
Reply
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jun '25
@Belynda Scarsbrook No, don't create custom categories. Google's category list is fixed.
"Re-theming" means you pick the closest available category from Google's list, then optimize your content for what you actually want to rank for.
Example: Want to rank for "solicitor" but closest GBP category is "lawyer" - use lawyer category, but write content targeting "solicitor [city]."
Stick with what Google offers. Use your content to bridge the gap between available categories and what Australians actually search for.
1
Reply
2
Belynda Scarsbrook
• Jun '25
Perfect, thanks for clarifying @Caleb Ulku
0
Reply
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jun '25
@Belynda Scarsbrook Check out the retheming lesson to make sure you don't miss the details on making sure Google understands you're still in the right category and you're maintaining the consistency =)
2
Reply
2
Belynda Scarsbrook
• Jun '25
@Caleb Ulku thank you, I came across this as continued on through the course :)

Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jun '25
I don’t think so. It’s a lot and pool cleaner isn’t usually super competitive. Definitely watch the rank map as you’re building all that out so you know when to stop :)
0
Reply
2
Joshua Albanese
• Jun '25
@Caleb Ulku Do you find that once you write all the pages for a niche that you can reuse the pages and change the copy to fit that next company? What are your thoughts on it?
0
Reply
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jun '25
@Joshua Albanese Yes, template the structure and process, not the actual content.
Same business type = same Core 30 framework. “Plumber [City]” structure works for every plumber client.
But write unique content for each client using AI. Can’t copy-paste paragraphs across different domains - Google hates duplicate content.
Template: keyword targeting, internal linking, page structure
Unique: actual written content, business details, local references
Use the same proven framework but generate fresh content each time. That’s how you scale efficiently without duplicate content penalties. =)
2
Reply
1

Often momentum. Google uses goal completion. If they answer the phone and are polite so callers don’t call other businesses, they’ll often stay in the top 3
Honestly this is a big part of why I don’t have a shred subscription anymore. Competitor analysis was driving me and my team insane so we just stopped doing it. In last summer’s leak, Google showed it’s watching like 30k factors or something like that. Very difficult to answer “why is this company ranking and not mine”

Here are few options for H2 tags that include "medical office"
Our Modern Medical Office in [City Name]
State-of-the-Art Medical Office Equipment
What to Expect at Our Medical Office
Medical Office Hours and Location

Their goal is to rank the GBPs but maint... See more

•Financial Planner Chicago
•Financial Planner Los Angeles

Reply
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jun '25
@Steve Mizer You figured it out. Each location gets full Core 30 structure. 2 locations = Core 60 total.
Homepage = "Financial Planner" (or other general/national search query)
Location pages = "Financial Planner Chicago" and "Financial Planner Los Angeles"
Homepage stays general for your national brand/ads. Location pages handle local rankings.
Don't try to rank the homepage for local terms when you're going national. Keep them separate.

Caleb Ulku

@Martijn Sam Skip Wikipedia links.
Getting Wikipedia links is hard, maintaining them is harder, and they don’t help local SEO anyway.
Focus on local sponsorships instead: Chamber of Commerce, youth sports, local nonprofits. Much easier to get and actually relevant for local businesses.
Don’t waste time chasing Wikipedia when you can sponsor a little league team for $200 and get a permanent link.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-attributes-not-showing
2
Daniel Namie
Jun '25 • SEO
GBP Attributes not showing
Just switched over to Lead Snap and I think it helped identify a problem with one of my clients. Take a look at the attached image. Categories are not showing, main image, reviews, review count etc.. What could be causing this problem?
Like
0
3 comments
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jun '25
Look at the live listing in an incognito browser and on Maps.
If categories, photos or reviews are also gone publicly, it’s almost always a Google-side issue (soft suspension, pending re-verification, or a temporary bug).
If everything looks normal to the public and the problem only shows inside LeadSnap, it’s almost certainly an API/permissions hiccup inside the tool and I'd suggest reaching out to their support.

Not uncommon at all. Adding 5 additional categories will often trigger a re-verification like this, especially for a category like life insurance agent which typically falls under YMYL.
Just a soft review because it's still visible on search (assuming you verified that with an incognito search or similar)
Don't make any other changes until you see the status change.
Prep what you need now in case a video re-verification is necessary. Google is reviewing it now. You might receive a request for more ... See more

Hey Matt. I'm in Denver and have a similar situation with my client who is in a suburb. 7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jun '25 (edited)
You're not going to rank Indianapolis while your address is in a suburb. I mean it's possible. But it will be much faster and easier to get a new GBP with an Indianapolis address. Proximity is an important rank factor and changing cities in the address is a massive negative signal for proximity. The amount of trust and relevance you need to overcome that proximity issue is massive.
In my 10 years, I've seen ranking in a main city while located in a suburb successfully done a handful of times. But... See more

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/schema-question-secondary-category-and-service-pages
2
Christy Schlothauer
Jun '25 (edited) • SEO
Schema Question - Secondary Category and Service Pages
@Caleb Ulku What type of page do the secondary category and service pages count as? They will get the organization schema, FAQ, and review schema too, when appropriate. Can they also be treated as an article page? Thanks!
Like
0
1 comment
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jun '25
@Christy Schlothauer
Service and category pages aren’t articles - they’re service pages.
Use organization schema + local business schema (if it’s the GBP landing page) + FAQ/review schema when appropriate.
Don’t use article schema on service pages. That’s for blog posts and supporting content.
Keep it simple - service pages sell services, articles provide information.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
3
Reply
1

Christy Schlothauer
Jun '25 • SEO
Geographic Coverage Question
When topical relevance is strong but you need broader geographic coverage:
Target Keywords: [Primary Category/Service] + [Geographic Landmark]
Examples:
Purpose: Expand your visibility across the service area
My question: For a service based business (I know it's not ideal!) but can I build out additional City pages for this step? I would pick the top cities in my service area that I want to rank.

Like
0
5 comments
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jun '25
You sure can. Won't help you rank in them
Even for a service area business with no visible address, Google knows what address you used to verify the original GBP and it's going to be very hard to rank outside of that address
You can move the pin to other locations, but then you lose the ability to rank where you were. Plus, moving the pin will often trigger a re-verification

@Christy Schlothauer Distance is based on your competition and how much relevance and trust you've built.
More competition means you'll need to build more trust and authority to overcome the proximity factor.
This is 113 sq miles centered in downtown Chicago (the miles counts the area in the lake with no marked positions so actually maybe half that) in a highly competitive space. Definitely possible to rank far away if the city is the same

===POST=== /ai-seo-mastery-pro/link-placement
3
Andy Fladung
Jun '25 • SEO
Link placement
I am hoping to get further clarification from one of your videos. Does it matter where I place the internal link on a page? For example, if I have the primary category page and create a link to the home page, does it matter where I place the link in order to get the maximum juice?
Like
0
4 comments
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jun '25
As long as it’s not the header, footer, or side bar no it doesn’t matter
0
Reply
3
Andy Fladung
• Jun '25
Thank you.
0
Reply
3
Jeremy Bengtson
• Jun '25 (edited)
@Andy Fladung Caleb’s advice is directionally correct -caleb is never wrong- (links in body copy all behave similarly) but experts add two nuances:
Site-wide nav/footer links still pass PageRank, just typically less topical context and fewer user clicks.
Inside the body, it’s anchor text and topical relevance—not pixel height—that move the needle most. https://www.seroundtable.com/location-of-internal-links-on-a-page-matter-for-seo-32838.html
0
Reply
3
Destynnie Berard
• Jun '25 (edited)
Check out internal link anchor text guidelines for the different types of links. But generally, no, as long as it's in the body content, you're fine. (i.e., in the page or article content somewhere)
Footer, header, and side bar are generally discounted, so avoid those.
0

External Links Schedule
I have watched the videos on link building and understand about getting the different links and one per new page. I have 3 ready to go.
If there like a set system or flow you follow @Caleb Ulku when a page is published its like you do these 3 things as far as links from these 3 places?
I am 50 videos in on a 100 videos in 100 days goal for my client. We are turning each one into a blog post to link back to 2 tier page and homepage.
Thanks for the help.
Like
1
4 comments
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jun '25
Once a new piece of content / uRL / article (whatever you want to call it) is published, we source the link.
Honestly, blogs don't really move the needle much in my experience. It's much more effecitve to build a website structure that matches the GBP first, then write specific supporting content based on the local rank map where necessary
If your video topics are supporting content based on the local rank map, then cool
But general blog posts don't do much any more after AI made content free.
0

3
David Dutton

Reply
7
Caleb Ulku
👑
• Jun '25
Beyond that, we do the press release / blog comments / social signals every few months
And 2-3 per month of the powerful hyper local links to the GBP landing page until the site has built sufficient trust. We know it has sufficient trust when the local rank maps looks good
Everything is based on the local rank map
